Japanese: 同日　某時刻 おぼろ橋
English: February 9 Dusky Bridge

Japanese: ‥‥急に、だれにも 会わなくなっちまったな。
English: It's weird that there's no one around all of a sudden.

Japanese: 人がいないと、ホントに 静かだな、山の中は。
English: It sure gets quiet up here in the mountains when you're all on your own.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、この先に 山小屋があったけど‥‥
English: Speaking of alone... I guess I should go check out the shack just down this path...

Japanese: 矢張のヤツ、またあそこで ふてくされてるのだろうか。
English: Maybe I'll find Larry there sulking again.

Japanese: 《おぼろ橋》というより、本当に 《おんぼろ橋》になっちまった。
English: Now it really is more of a "Dusty Bridge" than a "Dusky Bridge".

Japanese: １０年ほど前の誘拐事件といい、 呪われた橋、だよな‥‥
English: That kidnapping incident happened here 11 years ago, so maybe it's cursed, too.

Japanese: 葉桜院には、電話がないらしい。
English: Hazakura Temple doesn't have a single private phone line...

Japanese: このへんに民家はないから、 ビキニさん専用、なのだろうか。
English: ...and there aren't any houses in the area, so I guess Bikini is the only one who uses this.

Japanese: ‥‥携帯電話ぐらい、 もってくればよかったな。
English: ...I really wish I had brought my cell phone with me.

Japanese: あいかわらず、危なっかしく かたむいている。
English: That thing looks like it's ready to collapse... At least more than it did before.

Japanese: ビキニさんの話では、吾童山は 地震の多い土地柄らしい。
English: According to Bikini, Eagle Mountain is very prone to earthquakes.

Japanese: イヤな予感がしたけど‥‥ 現実になってしまった。
English: I had a feeling something bad was going to happen, and sure enough, it did.

Japanese: 御剣のヤツ、このあたりには 住めそうもないな。
English: Yup. Edgeworth definitely couldn't live up here!

Japanese: 葉桜院に向かう方向とは別に、 １本の細い道がのびている。
English: There's a narrow path going off in a different direction than that of the Main Hall.

Japanese: 道しるべには《極楽庵へ》と 書かれているようだ。
English: Looks like someone's taken the effort to write "To Heavenly Hall" on the signpost.

Japanese: 向こう岸まで、２０メートル近く ありそうだ。
English: It looks like it's about 20 yards to that cliff over there.

Japanese: こちらから、奥の院は見えない。
English: But I can't see the Inner Temple from here.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃん、ブジだといいけど‥‥
English: I really hope Maya's OK...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 山小屋　極楽庵
English: February 9 Heavenly Hall

Japanese: あ！　なるほどくん！
English: Oh! Mr. Nick!

Japanese: 春美ちゃん！ 何してるの、こんなところで。
English: Pearls! What are you doing here?

Japanese: そ、それより‥‥ 真宵さまはっ！　どうですかっ！
English: N-Nothing... What about Mystic Maya!? Is she alright!?

Japanese: え。‥‥うん。それが、まだ‥‥
English: Um... Well... We don't know yet.

Japanese: そ、そうなのですか‥‥
English: Oh... I see...

Japanese: おい、コラ！ ナニしに来たんだよ、成歩堂！
English: Hey! What do you think you're doing here, Nick!?

Japanese: 矢張‥‥
English: Larry...

Japanese: ここは、負け犬たちがつどう、 負け犬たちの山小屋だぜ！
English: This is the losers' shack, where losers get together to lose themselves!

Japanese: まけいぬ‥‥？
English: This is the what...?

Japanese: 負け犬たちが身を寄せあって なぐさめあうんだよ！　悪いか！
English: Hey, we find comfort in each other's failures, OK? You got a problem with that?

Japanese: ‥‥わたくし、マシスさんに 似顔絵を描いていただきました。
English: Look, Mr. Nick. Mr. Laurice did a picture of me!

Japanese: よ、よかったね。
English: That's, um... great, Pearls.

Japanese: オレたち、これから たき木を集めに行くんだ！
English: We're going to gather firewood now.

Japanese: うらぶれたキャンプファイヤーで うす汚れたイモを焼くんだ！
English: We'll be cooking some half-rotten potatoes over a miserable little camp fire.

Japanese: ジャマするなよな！
English: So stay out of our way!

Japanese: （‥‥まいったな。すっかり 盛り上がってるぞ‥‥逆向きに）
English: (I don't believe this... Why can't he try getting fired up over becoming a better man?)

Japanese: どうせ、オレの言うことなんて ダレも信じちゃくれねえんだ！
English: No one believes a word I say anymore.

Japanese: ハルミちゃん！　ダメだぜ、 こんなヤツを信用しちゃあ！
English: Listen to me, Pearl. You don't wanna trust this kind of guy, OK? He'll only let you down!

Japanese: まよいさま‥‥
English: Oh, Mystic Maya...

Japanese: ワラぶきの屋根に、 雪がふんわりとつもっている。
English: The snow is really piled up on the straw roof.

Japanese: おとぎ話の世界みたいだ。 ‥‥屋根だけ見れば。
English: It's otherworldly... Like something out of a fairy tale. ...Well, the roof part anyway.

Japanese: 《極楽庵》と書かれた 一枚板が、かかげられている。
English: The sign says, "Heavenly Hall".

Japanese: こんなサムいところで 一晩すごしたら‥‥
English: I guess it's pretty fitting. If you spent a night in this little freezing shack...

Japanese: たしかに、極楽あたりに 行けそうな気がする。
English: ...you'd probably find yourself at the pearly gates by morning.

Japanese: ‥‥せっかくのいいフンイキが 台なしになっている。
English: That stuff is really ruining the beautiful atmosphere around here.

Japanese: この、なんとも イタイタしい感じは‥‥
English: It's so inappropriate.

Japanese: あえてたとえるならば、 ビキニをつけたビキニさん、かな。
English: Like Sister Bikini in a bikini! *shudder* ...I've gotta think of the kittens...

Japanese: これをのぼっていくと、 おぼろ橋のたもとにつく。
English: These steps lead up to the foot of Dusky Bridge.

Japanese: おりて来るのはラクでいいけど‥‥ 帰るのが、めんどくさいな。
English: Coming down is a cinch, but going back up doesn't look like a walk in the park.

Japanese: ‥‥川がなぜ下流に向かうのか、 水のキモチがわかる気がする。
English: I'm starting to understand why rivers always want to flow downstream, not up.

Japanese: あれは‥‥おぼろ橋だな。 遠いけど、ハッキリ見える。
English: That's Dusky Bridge up there. It's amazing how clearly you can see it from so far away.

Japanese: 矢張のヤツ、小屋の窓から あの事件を目撃したのだろう。
English: Larry supposedly saw everything through the shack's window.

Japanese: このへんは明かりがないから、 夜はまっ暗だろうけど‥‥
English: Not much light gets down here, so it must be pitch black at night...

Japanese: あの晩にかぎっては、 橋がバリバリ燃えてたからなあ。
English: But this area was probably illuminated that night because of the burning bridge above.

Japanese: これが、ウワサの吾童川か。 事件当夜、ぼくも流されてみた。
English: This is the famous Eagle River. I got to try out its rough rapids the other night.

Japanese: 《かなりの急流だ》とは 聞いていたけど‥‥
English: They say it has a really strong current.

Japanese: たしかに、そうとうなパワー、 そしてスピードだったな、アレは。
English: And they weren't kidding. It's both powerful and fast-flowing.

Japanese: よく助かったな、ぼく。
English: I was dead lucky... I mean lucky not to be dead!

Japanese: 小屋のドテっぱらに、 好き勝手にラクガキしてある。
English: Look at this graffiti, scrawled right on the front of the shack.

Japanese: ‥‥でも、アイツの似顔絵、 意外と似ているんだよな。
English: At least he managed to get a pretty good likeness.

Japanese: 考えてみれば、あいつには ゲージュツが合うのかもしれない。
English: When I really think about it, I guess art kind of suits him.

Japanese: 存在自体がアートだからな。 ‥‥あるイミ。
English: I mean, his very existence is an art... of sorts.

Japanese: ‥‥矢張。この絵について なんか言いたいコト、あるか？
English: Larry, is there something you want to tell me about this picture?

Japanese: ヘン！　もお、オマエに 言うコトなんか、ナニもねえぜ！
English: Heh! I've got nothing to say to you, Nick!

Japanese: オレと春美ちゃんは、負け犬どうし ここでイモ食って暮らすんだ！
English: My life's here now, with Pearl. Two losers cooking potatoes together forevermore!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (What am I going to do with him...?)

Japanese: じゃ、春美ちゃん。 この絵なんだけど‥‥どう思う？
English: Alright then. What do you think about this picture, Pearls?

Japanese: まあ‥‥とても、 おじょうずですね！
English: I... I think it's really well drawn!

Japanese: わたくし、お絵かきは ニガテなものですから‥‥
English: I can't draw at all, so I think it's really amazing.

Japanese: だろ！　だろ！　この火の色が なかなか出なくてさー。
English: See! Someone appreciates it! It's tough getting the flames to look like that, you know.

Japanese: お空をお飛びになってるのは あやめさま、でしょうか？
English: It's supposed to be Sister Iris flying through the air, isn't it?

Japanese: ‥‥ユメいっぱいの、 とてもステキな絵だと思います！
English: I love it! It's like a dream. A wonderful fantasy.

Japanese: いやいや、ユメじゃねえの！
English: No, no, no! It wasn't a dream!

Japanese: ホントに飛んだワケよ！ あの晩、あやめちゃんは。
English: She really flew! I'm telling you, Iris really flew that night.

Japanese: まあ！ ‥‥ま、マシスさま‥‥
English: But Mr. Laurice...

Japanese: う。
English: *sigh*

Japanese: は‥‥ハルミちゃんまで そんな目で‥‥
English: Not you too... Please don't look at me like that, Pearl...

Japanese: カワイソウなヒトを見る目で オレを見ないでくれェェェ！
English: Don't look at me like I'm some kind of nutcase. I beg of you!

Japanese: （やっぱり、矢張はホンキで 描いたんだな。この絵‥‥）
English: (I guess this picture really is a representation of what Larry thinks he saw...)

Japanese: あの晩‥‥たしかにオレ、 ここでウトウトしてたけど‥‥
English: OK, so I was half asleep when I was here that night.

Japanese: カミナリの音で、ビックリして 目がさめたんだ！　そして‥‥
English: But I was wide awake after the lightning struck!

Japanese: ホントに、見たんだよ！ この、スゴいシーンを！
English: And I saw what I saw! It was exactly like I drew in the picture.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ 信じるしかない、のか‥‥？）
English: (And it looks like I don't have any choice but to take this sketch at face value...)

Japanese: なんだよ、最後の“？”は！
English: Hey! What's with the look of doubt on your face!?

Japanese: あの‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: Umm, Pearls...

Japanese: は、はい。なんでしょうか。
English: Y-Yes, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: 事件があった夜の ことなんだけど‥‥
English: I've been meaning to ask you about the night of the murder...

Japanese: 春美ちゃん、どこで何をしてたの？ 事件があったころ。
English: Where were you, and what exactly were you doing when it all happened?

Japanese: あ‥‥あの。ええと‥‥
わた、わた、わたくしごときモノの コトなんて‥‥
English: I was just... Well... I'm just a kid, you know.

Japanese: どどどどどどどうでもよろしいじゃ ありませんか！
English: I-I'm sure it doesn't really matter wh-what I was doing, d-d-does it, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: ‥‥そういうわけには いかないんだよ。
English: Sorry, Pearls... But yes, it does.

Japanese: 事件が起こった晩‥‥
English: On the night of the murder...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、部屋でエリス先生と 絵本を読んでいたはずだった。
English: ...you were supposed to be in Ms. Deauxnim's room, reading a book together.

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: エリス先生は殺害されて、 春美ちゃんも、奥の院にいた。
English: Ms. Deauxnim was murdered, and you, Pearls, were at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: う、ううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: いったい、あの晩‥‥ 何があったのかな？
English: Just what exactly happened that night, Pearls...?

Japanese: 申しわけありません。 ‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: I'm really sorry, Mr. Nick...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥わるい子です。 だから、きっと‥‥
English: I... I've been a bad girl.

Japanese: バチが当たったのですね。
English: I know I'll be punished.

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？ 春美ちゃん。
English: What are you talking about, Pearls?

Japanese: わたくしの霊力が‥‥ 消えてしまったのです。
English: My spiritual power... It has disappeared.
(What? Her powers have disappeared...?)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。 教えてくれないかな。
English: Come on, Pearls. You can tell me.

Japanese: 事件があった夜‥‥ いったい、何があったのか？
English: What happened to you on the night of the murder?

Japanese: なぜ、春美ちゃんは今日、 奥の院で保護されたか‥‥？
English: Why were you found taking refuge at the Inner Temple today?

Japanese: わ‥‥わたくし‥‥ I... I...
（‥‥よっぽどのヒミツが あるらしいな‥‥）
English: (Wow. It must be a really big secret she's hiding.)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。 霊力が消えた、というのは‥‥？
English: What do you mean your power has disappeared, Pearls?

Japanese: わたくし、もう‥‥ おしまいです。
English: It's... It's all over for me.

Japanese: 霊の呼べない霊媒師なんて‥‥ 絵の描けない画家さまのよう‥‥
English: A spirit medium who can't channel spirits is... like a painter who can't paint.

Japanese: なんで、そこでオレを見るんだよ！
English: Hey! What are you looking at me for!?

Japanese: もしかして、春美ちゃん‥‥
English: Pearls.

Japanese: この葉桜院で、だれかの霊を 呼ぼうとして‥‥
English: Did you try to channel someone's spirit right here at Hazakura Temple?

Japanese: ‥‥失敗、したのかな？
English: Tried but failed, perhaps?
Ah...

Japanese: うわああああああああああああん！
English: Waaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: コラァ、なるほどお！ 女の子を泣かすな！
English: Way to go, Nick! You made her cry!

Japanese: もお、オレも泣いちゃうぞ！ うおおおおおおおおおおおおおん！
English: That's... That was really c-cruel of you... Waaaaaaaah!

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Just as I thought...)

Japanese: （サイコ・ロックを解除するしか ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (I'm going to have to break her Psyche-Lock to get the truth out of her...)

Japanese: あの、春美ちゃん‥‥？
English: Um, Pearls...?

Japanese: （‥‥ダメだ）
English: (...It's no good.)

Japanese: （今は、何も目に入らない 状況みたいだ‥‥）
English: (She's in no condition to look at anything for me right now.)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 山小屋　極楽庵
English: February 9 Heavenly Hall

Japanese: ‥‥あれ。春美ちゃんと 矢張、いないぞ。
English: Hmm... I was expecting Larry and Pearls to be here.

Japanese: たき木を集めに 行っちまったのかな。
English: I guess they must've gone to collect firewood.

Japanese: ‥‥しかたない。 あとでまた、来てみよう。
English: I suppose I'll have to check back later.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 山小屋　極楽庵
English: February 9 Heavenly Hall

Japanese: （お。小屋の前で、あのふたりが たき火をしてるぞ‥‥）
English: (Look at that! They really did make a fire, right in front of the shack...)

Japanese: ‥‥あ、なるほどくん！
English: Oh! Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ハッ！ 一歩おそかったな、成歩堂！
English: Hah! You're too late, Nick!

Japanese: イモならもう、 ゼンブ食っちまったぜ！
English: If you came here for one of our potatoes, we've already polished them all off!

Japanese: （‥‥手がかりは ほぼ、そろった‥‥）
English: (I think I've pretty much got all the evidence I need.)

Japanese: （そろそろ、春美ちゃんの 話を聞くときがきたかな‥‥）
English: (Now I just need to find out what Pearls is hiding.)

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとう、春美ちゃん。 おかげで、助かったよ。
English: Thanks, Pearls. You really helped me out.

Japanese: どういたしまして！
English: You're very welcome!

Japanese: わたくしどもには、 おかあさまがついています！
English: My mother is watching over us!

Japanese: きっと‥‥真宵さまも ブジですよね！
English: So I'm sure Mystic Maya will be alright!

Japanese: （この、無邪気な笑顔‥‥）
English: (Look at that innocent smile on her face.)

Japanese: （‥‥ぼくは、なんと言えば いいんだろう‥‥？）
English: (What am I supposed to say to her?)

Japanese: クッ‥‥！
アンタにも、やっと わかったかい？　まるほどう。
English: Finally figured it out, have you, Trite?

Japanese: ‥‥“知らない”ってコトが、 どれほど大きな《罪》なのか‥‥
English: You've finally realized how terrible of a crime being painfully oblivious is.

Japanese: ご‥‥、ゴドー検事！
English: G-Godot!

Japanese: 修験洞の入り口で、パズル遊びを しているみたいだな。
English: The entrance to the Sacred Cavern looks like a freaking puzzle workshop.

Japanese: （あやめさんのこと、か‥‥）
English: (I guess he's talking about Iris and the lock-breaking effort...)

Japanese: ‥‥ムダなことさ。
English: ...But it's all a waste of time.

Japanese: ど、どういうことですか‥‥？
English: W-Why do you say that?

Japanese: おそらく‥‥綾里　真宵は もう、帰ってこねえだろうぜ。
English: Because Maya Fey isn't coming back.
What?

Japanese: な、何を言うんだ！ なぜ、あなたがそんなことを‥‥
English: You don't know what you're talking about! How can you say something like that?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタが、守ってやる べきだったんだ。
English: It was your job to protect her, Trite.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵も‥‥ そして、綾里　千尋も。
English: Just like it was your job to protect Mia Fey...

Japanese: ‥‥２人は、大変な事件に 巻きこまれていたのさ。
English: Two sisters, caught up in the worst circumstances.

Japanese: そ‥‥それは わかりましたけど‥‥
English: I... I realize that, but...

Japanese: いつも、そばにいたのは‥‥ アンタだけ、だったんだ。
English: And you were the only one who was by their sides.

Japanese: 助けてやれたのは、 アンタだけだったんだよ！
English: You were the only one who could have saved them.

Japanese: で、でも‥‥！ ぼくは、何も知らなかった‥‥
English: But I didn't know anything about what was going on!

Japanese: クッ‥‥！ 言っただろう？　まるほどう。
English: Ha...! What did I just say, Trite?

Japanese: “知らない”ってのは、 大きな《罪》なんだ、と‥‥
English: Being oblivious is a heinous crime in itself.

Japanese: 明日、だ。
English: Tomorrow...

Japanese: すべての決着をつけてやるぜ。 ‥‥法廷で、な。
English: We'll settle everything in court tomorrow. ...Once and for all.

Japanese: な‥‥なるほどくん‥‥
English: M-Mr. Nick...

Japanese: い、今の‥‥検事さんの お話は‥‥まさか！
English: Is... Is what that man just said true?

Japanese: ‥‥だいじょうぶだよ、 春美ちゃん。
English: It'll be alright, Pearls.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは、きっと生きている。 ‥‥明日になれば、わかるさ！
English: I'm sure Maya's alive. You'll see for yourself tomorrow!

Japanese: ‥‥そ‥‥ そうですよね！
English: I... Yeah! That's right!

Japanese: わたくし‥‥なるほどくんを 信じていますから！
English: I know I can trust you, Mr. Nick!

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん‥‥
English: ...My dear Pearl...

Japanese: ‥‥よくやってくれましたね、 アタクシの春美ちゃん‥‥
English: ...You've done so well, my child. My Pearl...

Japanese: ‥‥あなたは、何も 知らなくてよいのですよ‥‥
English: ...It's better that you don't know...

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシには、この日が 来るのがわかっていました‥‥
English: ...I knew this day would come for you...

Japanese: ‥‥綾里家・本家の血は‥‥ 絶えたのです。
English: ...The blood of the main family is no more...

Japanese: ‥‥今度こそ‥‥あなたが。 家元の座を手にする‥‥
English: ...Now you... You are to take the place at the head of the Fey clan...

Japanese: ‥‥アタクシの、最後のねがい‥‥ ‥‥間に合ったようですね‥‥
English: ...My last, great wish... It seems I was just in time...

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥あの晩、お夕食のあと 奥の院に向かったのです。
English: I... After dinner that night, I did go to the Inner Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥あまったカレーの おナベを持って。
English: With a pot full of the leftover gravy.

Japanese: カレー‥‥か。
English: The gravy?

Japanese: 奥の院の掛け軸は 見ておりましたので‥‥
English: I saw the picture on the hanging scroll near the Sacred Cavern.

Japanese: お手紙に書いてあった《家元》とは きっと、このことだと思って‥‥
English: I was sure it was the Master of Kurain, like it said in the letter.

Japanese: ‥‥なるほどね。それで？
English: I see. And then?

Japanese: 雪のせいで、奥の院についたら １０時をとっくにすぎていました。
English: Well, it was already way past 10 when I got there because of all the snow.

Japanese: だから‥‥わたくし、奥の院の 《はなれ》へ行ったのです。
English: So I went to the Inner Temple guest area.
The guest area?

Japanese: ええ。そこで、修行が終わるのを お待ちしようと思いました。
English: Yes. I thought I could wait there until the training was over.

Japanese: ‥‥どうして《修験堂》に 行かなかったの？
English: Why didn't you just go to the Training Hall?

Japanese: 修験者の《本行》が始まったら、 立ち入りはユルされないのです。
English: Because Mystic Maya's main training had already started, and I couldn't interrupt it!

Japanese: そこで、真宵さまのブジを お祈りしていたのですが‥‥
English: So I just stayed where I was and prayed for her to get through it.

Japanese: その‥‥わたくし‥‥。
English: But then... I...

Japanese: ‥‥もしかして‥‥ 眠っちゃった、のかな？
English: Pearls, did you fall asleep?

Japanese: も、申しわけありません！ このような、だらしのない‥‥
English: I'm sorry! I didn't mean to! I just couldn't help it!

Japanese: いやいや、ハルミちゃん！ そりゃ、しかたねえって！
English: Hey, don't worry about it, Pearl! Who cares if you fell asleep?

Japanese: オレも、あやめちゃんを待ってて ケッキョク、寝ちまったし。
English: I fell asleep waiting for Iris, too. It happens.

Japanese: それで‥‥閉じこめられちゃった わけだね。奥の院に。
English: Anyway... Then you found yourself trapped at the Inner Temple?

Japanese: 目がさめたら、朝でした。 わたくし、泣いてしまいました！
English: Yes. When I woke up, it was morning. I tried not to cry, but...

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋はなくなっていて、 修験堂には、どなたもいなくて。
English: ...Dusky Bridge wasn't there anymore and there was no one in the Training Hall.

Japanese: お寝坊したから、置いていかれて しまったのだと思って‥‥
English: I thought everyone had left me because I overslept!

Japanese: お手紙を燃やして、いっしょに カレーをあたためて‥‥
English: I threw the letter into the incinerator, then I heated up the leftover gravy, and...

Japanese: （‥‥泣きながら 掛け軸にぶちまけたわけか）
English: (And let off some steam by chucking the gravy on the scroll while you cried...?)

Japanese: ‥‥タイヘンだったなあ、 ハルミちゃん。
English: It must've been pretty scary for you, Pearl.

Japanese: オレも小さいころ、よく寝坊して 成歩堂に置いていかれたよ。学校。
English: I know what it's like. Nick used to leave me behind when I fell asleep at school, too.

Japanese: ‥‥いっしょにするなよ。
English: Don't equate something so trivial with her experience, Larry.

Japanese: おかあさまのお手紙に 書いてあったのです。
English: It was written right in my mother's letter.

Japanese: 『《消灯の鐘》が聞こえたら、 すぐに霊媒をしなさい』‥‥と。
English: It said, "As soon as you hear the lights out bell, you must channel her spirit."

Japanese: 鐘の音が聞こえたとき、わたくし 奥の院に向かう途中でしたが‥‥
English: I was on my way over to the Inner Temple when I heard the bell ring.

Japanese: 呼び出したわけだね。 ‥‥《美柳　ちなみ》の霊を。
English: So you channeled Dahlia Hawthorne's spirit?

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: No...

Japanese: わたくし、できなかったのです！
English: I tried, but I couldn't do it!

Japanese: できなかった‥‥？
English: You couldn't do it...?

Japanese: こんなことは、初めてです。 霊を呼べない、なんて‥‥
English: I've never failed at channeling someone. This is the first time it's happened.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥それから、 何度も何度もやってみました。
English: I tried and I tried and I tried...

Japanese: きのうも‥‥そして、今朝も 試してみました！　でも‥‥
English: Yesterday, this morning, the whole time!

Japanese: どうしても、呼べないのです。
English: But I just couldn't do it...!

Japanese: だ、大丈夫だよ、ハルミちゃん！ 気を落としちゃいけねえ！
English: D-Don't let it get you down, Pearl. It'll all work out.

Japanese: オレでよければ！　いつだって 飛んでくるぜ！　ピョーン、って！
English: If you want, I'll come flying through the sky for you! Whooosh! Just like that!

Japanese: （《霊を呼べなかった》‥‥か）
English: (So she never managed to channel the spirit...)

Japanese: だから《霊力が消えた》‥‥ そう思ったんだ。
English: Is that why you think your spiritual powers are gone?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。わたくし、 もう‥‥どうしていいのか‥‥
English: Yes. I... I don't know what to do...

Japanese: 他に‥‥何か、考えられないの？ 霊が呼べない理由は。
English: Isn't there any other explanation for why you couldn't channel a spirit?

Japanese: １つだけ、ありますけど‥‥ ありそうもないことですから。
English: I suppose there's one other possibility. It's not very likely, though...

Japanese: ‥‥教えてほしいな。
English: Could you please tell me what it is anyway?

Japanese: わたくしより先に、だれかが その霊を呼んでいる場合です。
English: It could happen if someone else was already channeling the same spirit.

Japanese: “だれかが”‥‥？
English: Someone else? What do you mean?

Japanese: ‥‥はい。 霊体は、１つきりですから。
English: Well there's only one of each spirit, right...?

Japanese: 貸出中のレンタルビデオを 借りることはできないぜ、成歩堂！
English: Yup, it's like dating a girl, Nick. You can't see a hot chick if she's already taken!

Japanese: （‥‥事件があった晩‥‥）
English: (Then that would mean on the night of the murder...)

Japanese: （春美ちゃんより先に、だれかが 霊媒した、というのか‥‥？）
English: (...someone else channeled her spirit before Pearls could.)

Japanese: （美柳　ちなみの霊を‥‥）
English: (Someone else channeled the spirit of Dahlia Hawthorne!)

Japanese: わたくし、もう情けなくて。
English: I'm a failure.

Japanese: おかあさまの‥‥最後のおねがいも 聞けなかった、なんて‥‥
English: I couldn't even grant my mother's final wish...

Japanese: “最後のおねがい”‥‥？
English: Her "final wish"...?

Japanese: その、お手紙です。
English: Yes. This letter.

Japanese: おかあさま、おっしゃいました。 『これが、最後のおねがい』‥‥
English: "This is my last wish," she said.

Japanese: 『だから‥‥かならず、書いてある とおりにしなさい』‥‥と。
English: "So make sure you follow the instructions carefully."

Japanese: （‥‥そうだ。この、手紙‥‥）
English: (Hmm... This letter...)

Japanese: （もっと、詳しくハナシを 聞いておくか‥‥）
English: (I definitely need to find out more about it.)

Japanese: おかあさまは今《けいむしょ》と いうところに、いらっしゃいます。
English: My mother has gone to a place called a "penitentiary".

Japanese: う‥‥うん、そうだね。
English: Yeah... I know, Pearls.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥毎月、《めんかい》に 行くのですが‥‥
English: I visit her every month...

Japanese: あれは、先月のことでした。 おかあさま、おっしゃったのです。
English: And last month, she told me...

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。いよいよ、 《そのとき》が来たようです‥‥
English: The time we've been waiting for has come, Pearl.

Japanese: ‥‥あなたに、やっていただきたい ことがあります‥‥
English: There's something I need you to do for me.

Japanese: ‥‥ここに来る前、お屋敷に お手紙をかくしておきました。
English: I hid a letter for you at our home before they brought me here.

Japanese: ‥‥それをよく読んで、その とおりに行動してくれますね‥‥
English: I want you to read it, and do exactly what it says.

Japanese: ‥‥すべては、綾里家のため。 よいことなのです、春美ちゃん‥‥
English: It's for the good of the Fey clan, my angel. You'll be doing a great thing.

Japanese: ‥‥お手紙の、かくし場所は‥‥
English: Now, listen carefully, and I'll tell you where the letter's hidden...

Japanese: おかあさまは、いつだって やさしくて‥‥大好きです！
English: My mother is always nice to me... I love her very much!

Japanese: うんうん。母親ってのは、子どもの ためなら、何だってするからなぁ！
English: Yeah. Moms will do anything for their kids, right?

Japanese: “綾里家のため”‥‥もちろん わたくし、協力するつもりでした。
English: She said it was for the good of the Fey clan, so I knew I had to help her.

Japanese: だって‥‥それは、真宵さまの ためにもなるではないですか！
English: I mean, Mystic Maya's part of the Fey clan, so it had to be good for her, too.

Japanese: そうですよね！　なるほどくん。
English: That's right, isn't it, Mr. Nick?

Japanese: そ‥‥‥そう、だね。
English: I-I guess so, yeah...

Japanese: おかあさまのお手紙には、 お写真も入っていました。
English: There was a picture with her letter, too.

Japanese: 写真‥‥？
English: A picture?

Japanese: 美柳　ちなみさまの お写真です。
English: Of Ms. Dahlia Hawthorne.

Japanese: ‥‥それがあれば、霊媒は できるはずなのですが‥‥
English: Usually, a picture is enough to channel someone's spirit, but this time...

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、ヘンだなァ。
English: ...Pretty weird, huh?

Japanese: （テキトーにあいづちを 打っている‥‥）
English: (Like Larry knows anything about this stuff!)

Japanese: それに‥‥おかあさまの お手紙なのですが‥‥
English: There's something else that was strange about my mother's letter...

Japanese: だれかが一度、 封を破った跡がありました。
English: The seal on it was broken, as if someone had already opened it once before.

Japanese: ‥‥そりゃ、ヘンだなァ。
English: ...That sure is pretty strange.

Japanese: （封が‥‥破られていた‥‥？）
English: (Someone had opened it already?)

Japanese: 証拠品《燃えカス》の データを書きなおした。
English: Burnt Letter updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ～事件当夜のこと～
English: -- The Night of the Crime --

Japanese: 事件が起こった晩の、夕食後‥‥
English: After dinner on the night of the murder...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、エリス先生の部屋で 絵本を読むはずだったんだよね？
English: ...you were supposed to be in Elise Deauxnim's room, reading a book together, correct?

Japanese: ええ！　さそっていただいて、 わたくし、うれしくて！
English: Yes! I was so happy when she invited me!
But...

Japanese: わたくし‥‥けっきょく、 行けなかったのです。
English: I didn't go in the end.

Japanese: 行けなかった‥‥？
English: You didn't go...?

Japanese: どうしても、行かなければならない ところがありましたから。
English: No. There was somewhere else I had to go instead.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥もう、 シンパイでシンパイで！
English: I was so worried, I... I had to go.

Japanese: いても立っても いられなくなってしまったのです。
English: I didn't know what to do with myself. I was so nervous.

Japanese: （‥‥春美ちゃんは、エリス先生の 部屋へは行かなかった‥‥）
English: (So Pearls never went to Ms. Deauxnim's room...)

Japanese: （重大な“シンパイごと”が あったから‥‥）
English: (Because she was too worried about something or someone else...)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: ...Pearls.

Japanese: きっと‥‥あの晩、春美ちゃんは ここへ行ったんだね？
English: On the night of the murder, you went to this place, didn't you...?

Japanese: それは、ここだ！ ‥‥どうかな、春美ちゃん。
English: You went here, didn't you Pearls?

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥まだ、 打ち明けられないみたいだ）
English: (Looks like she's still not going to open up to me.)

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、“シンパイごと”が あるから、ここへ行ったんだね？
English: This is where you went because you were so worried, right?

Japanese: じゃあ、春美ちゃんが、何を “シンパイ”していたか‥‥
English: Then the next question is, "Who or what were you so worried about?"

Japanese: 次は、それを当ててみよう。
English: Now, I'm going to take a guess, and you can tell me if I'm right or wrong, OK?

Japanese: え！　‥‥そんなことが わかるのですかっ！
English: Y-You mean you know!?

Japanese: もし、当たったら‥‥ 正直に教えてくれるね？
English: If I'm right, will you tell me the whole truth?
O-OK...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、“そこ”へ行った 理由は、これしかないんだよ。
English: You went to this place for one very simple reason, and that is this:

Japanese: どうかな。これが、春美ちゃんの “シンパイごと”だろう？
English: Well Pearls? This is what you were worrying about, right?

Japanese: あの。わたくし、もっと シンパイなことがあります。
English: There's something I'm worrying about right now, actually.

Japanese: それは‥‥？
English: What's that?

Japanese: なるほどくんのことです。 おカオが、コゲ茶色ですから。
English: You, Mr. Nick. Your face has turned a really strange color.
Huh?

Japanese: ムリせず、お休みに なったほうがよろしいのでは‥‥
English: You shouldn't work so hard, you know. You need to take a break once in a while.

Japanese: （‥‥いたわられてしまった）
English: (...Being chided by a 9 year old. How embarrassing...)

Japanese: （この子が“シンパイ”することと いえば‥‥）
English: (But there's really only one thing Pearls could have been worried about that night.)

Japanese: （“彼女”のことしか ないじゃないか‥‥！）
English: (She must have been worried about "her". There's no one else it could be.)

Japanese: わたくし、おふとんを しいてきますね！
English: I'll go get some sheets to make up a bed for you.

Japanese: いやいや！　スキマ風の吹きすさぶ 山小屋で寝たら、死んじまうよ！
English: No, no, it's OK! I'll die of pneumonia if I nap in a drafty little shack like this!

Japanese: （もう一度‥‥最初から 考えなおすんだ！）
English: (I guess I'm going to have to think this through one more time from the very beginning.)

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが死ぬほど シンパイしてくれたのは‥‥
English: It's obvious what you were so worried about, Pearls.

Japanese: もちろん、真宵ちゃんのこと ‥‥だよね？
English: It was Maya, wasn't it?
Ah!

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんが申し込んだのは、 かなりキケンな修行だった。
English: You knew the training Maya was undertaking was dangerous.

Japanese: ‥‥なんと言っても、 《スペシャル・コース》だから。
English: After all, it was the Special Course.

Japanese: あたし、スペシャル・コースを 申し込んだんですよ！
English: I signed up for your "Special Course"!

Japanese: あらあらあらあら。 このサムいのにスペシャル？
English: Well, my, my, my. Quite brave of you considering how cold it is!

Japanese: イノチしらずねぇ、ワカい子は。
English: Young people can be so reckless with their health!

Japanese: 死んでも知らないから。 わはは。はは。わはははは。
English: Don't blame me if you become one with those you channel. Wa ha ha! Ho ho ho!

Japanese: ‥‥ビキニさんにオドかされて、 春美ちゃんはシンパイになった。
English: Sister Bikini scared you with what she said, didn't she?

Japanese: 葉桜院を紹介したのも、 春美ちゃんだったからね。
English: And because it was you who introduced Hazakura Temple to Maya in the first place...

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、セキニンを 感じたんじゃないかな？
English: ...you felt responsible, didn't you?

Japanese: いったい‥‥真宵さまが どうなってしまうのか‥‥
English: Thinking about what could happen to Mystic Maya made me more and more worried...

Japanese: わたくし、もう気が狂いそうに なってしまったのです。
English: I couldn't sit still at all.

Japanese: だから‥‥ようすを見るために、 奥の院へ‥‥
English: That's why I decided to go and find out how she was doing at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: あの晩、春美ちゃんが奥の院に 行ったのは、アキラカなんだ。
English: Then we're clear now that you went to the Inner Temple that night, Pearls.

Japanese: わからないのは‥‥ そのあと、何があったのか‥‥？
English: What's not clear is what happened after that...

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: Unngh...

Japanese: 春美ちゃんが、奥の院に行った のは‥‥何時ごろだったのかな？
English: About what time was it when you headed over to the Inner Temple?

Japanese: たぶん‥‥葉桜院を出たのは、 ９時半ぐらいだったと思います。
English: Um... It was probably around 9:30 when I left the Main Hall.

Japanese: 《本行》は、１０時ごろから 始まると聞いていましたので‥‥
English: I heard the real training was supposed to start at 10.

Japanese: その時間までに、奥の院に 行こうと思ったのです。でも‥‥
English: I wanted to get there before it started.

Japanese: 雪のせいで、奥の院につく前に、 １０時になってしまいました。
English: But there was so much snow, so I didn't get there until after 10 o'clock.

Japanese: １０時に‥‥？　どうして、 それがわかったのかな？
English: Until after 10? How did you know what time it was?

Japanese: あの‥‥。《消灯の鐘》が かすかに聞こえたのです。
English: Because I heard the bell ringing for lights out.

Japanese: （《消灯の鐘》か‥‥）
English: (She heard the lights out bell...?)

Japanese: ‥‥葉桜院の鐘つき堂、 とても小さいよね。
English: The Hazakura Temple bell's pretty small though, isn't it?

Japanese: よく、あの鐘の音が 聞こえたね。
English: You must have really good hearing.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥鐘の音には 注意していたのです。
English: I... I was really trying to pick up the sound of that bell, that's all.

Japanese: ‥‥聞き逃すわけには いきませんでしたから。
English: I didn't want to miss it. That would be terrible.

Japanese: （鐘の音を‥‥ 気にしていた‥‥？）
English: (She was trying to hear the bell...?)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: Tell me, Pearls...

Japanese: どうして、そんなに鐘の音を 気にしていたのかな？
English: Why were you so worried about hearing that bell?

Japanese: え‥‥！　そ、それは‥‥
English: Huh!? Oh! I...

Japanese: きっと、これのせいじゃないかな？ 鐘の音を聞き逃せなかった理由は。
English: I think I know why. The reason you were so worried about that bell was because of this:

Japanese: ‥‥あの、なるほどくん。今日は もう、お休みになったほうが。
English: You know, Mr. Nick, I think you should take a break now.
Huh?

Japanese: いつもトガっている髪の毛が 少し、うなだれています。
English: Even your hair's starting to look a bit tired. The spikes aren't looking so sharp.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥うなだれている らしいな。今の証拠）
English: (Looks like picking this piece of evidence wasn't so sharp of me, either.)

Japanese: ‥‥と、とにかく！
English: ...A-Anyway!

Japanese: あの晩、春美ちゃんは、鐘の音を 聞き逃すワケにはいかなかった！
English: You said you couldn't afford to miss the sound of the lights out bell...

Japanese: あの晩‥‥春美ちゃんには、 ある指示が与えられていた。
English: You were given some instructions to follow for that night, weren't you?

Japanese: きっと‥‥コイツに 見おぼえがあるんじゃないかな。
English: I presume you recognize these pieces of paper?

Japanese: ああああああッ！ そ、それを‥‥どこでッ！
English: Aaaaah! Wh-Where did you...?

Japanese: 奥の院の、焼却炉の中だよ。
English: In the incinerator at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: Pearls...

Japanese: きみはあの晩、このメモに 従っていたんじゃないかな？
English: You were following the instructions in this letter that night, weren't you?

Japanese: だから‥‥《消灯の鐘》を 気にしていたんだ。
English: That's why you couldn't afford to miss the sound of the lights out bell.

Japanese: す‥‥‥ すごいです、なるほどくん‥‥！
English: I... I'm speechless, Mr. Nick! You're amazing!

Japanese: ‥‥焼却炉から見つかった この《指示書》‥‥
English: These instructions that were found in the incinerator...

Japanese: やっぱり、春美ちゃんに 宛てられたもの、なのかな？
English: I believe they were written for you, Pearls.

Japanese: あ‥‥あの。わたくし、それはっ！
English: For me!? I... No...

Japanese: 見てのとおり、 ほとんど焼けちゃってるけど‥‥
English: As you can see, a large portion has been burnt...

Japanese: 最後の部分は、だいたい 読めるんだよ。
English: But the last section is still fairly legible.

Japanese: 『消灯の鐘が聞こえたら、 すぐに霊媒をしなさい』
English: "As soon as you hear the lights out bell, you must channel her spirit."

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、あの晩‥‥ だれかを霊媒したんだね？
English: Who was it, Pearls? Whose spirit were you supposed to be channeling?

Japanese: ‥‥ううう‥‥
English: Nngh...

Japanese: （《指示書》を書いた人物と、 その《目的》‥‥）
English: (Taking into account the author of the note, and their purpose for writing it...)

Japanese: （それを考えれば‥‥あの晩、 だれが霊媒されたのか？）
English: (...whose spirit would Pearls have been trying to channel?)

Japanese: （わかるはずだ！ むずかしくはない‥‥）
English: (Don't over-think it, Phoenix! It's pretty obvious who Pearls was supposed to channel...)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。あの晩、きみが 霊媒しようとした人物とは‥‥！
English: The person you were trying to channel that night, Pearls, was...

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな？　春美ちゃん。
English: Well, Pearls? Am I right?

Japanese: わたくし、もう‥‥ 霊媒なんて、できないのです。
English: I... I can't channel anyone anymore.

Japanese: 霊の呼べない霊媒師なんて‥‥ 絵の描けない画家さまのよう‥‥
English: A spirit medium who can't channel spirits... is like a painter who can't paint.

Japanese: （まいったな‥‥ 落ちこませてしまった）
English: (Poor kid. It's really getting her down.)

Japanese: でも‥‥わたくし。そのかたを 霊媒したことはありません！
English: But... I have never channeled that person's spirit!

Japanese: （‥‥手がかりは、もう じゅうぶんなハズだ）
English: (I should have all the evidence I need, so what am I not getting here...?)

Japanese: ‥‥《美柳　ちなみ》。それが、 指示書に書かれた名前だった。
English: It was Dahlia Hawthorne, wasn't it? That was the name that was in the letter.

Japanese: ああ‥‥ッ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: （やっぱり、そうだったか‥‥）
English: (It's just as I suspected...)

Japanese: （あの晩、奥の院で ビキニさんが会ったのは‥‥）
English: (It wasn't Iris who Sister Bikini met at the Inner Temple that night...)

Japanese: （あやめさんではなく、 美柳　ちなみだった‥‥）
English: (It was Dahlia Hawthorne!)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥ きみは、知ってるのかな？
English: Do you know anything about her, Pearls?

Japanese: 《美柳　ちなみ》が、 いったい、何者なのか‥‥？
English: Do you know what kind of a woman Dahlia Hawthorne was?

Japanese: ‥‥じつは、わたくし‥‥
English: Umm... no.

Japanese: 聞いたことがないのです。 美柳　ちなみさまというお名前‥‥
English: I've never heard of anyone by that name before reading those instructions...

Japanese: （やはり、知らないのか。 この、美柳　ちなみが‥‥）
English: (I thought so. Pearls doesn't have a clue.)

Japanese: （春美ちゃんの、お姉さんだ、 ってこと‥‥）
English: (She doesn't know that Dahlia is her sister...)

Japanese: 春美ちゃん。 《指示書》のことだけど‥‥
English: About the instructions in the letter I found, Pearls...

Japanese: いったい、だれが 書いたものなのかな？
English: Who wrote them?
Umm...

Japanese: 聞いたこともない 人物の霊を呼ぶなんて‥‥
English: Whoever it was asked you to channel the spirit of someone you'd never heard of...

Japanese: よっぽど、コイツには 《チカラ》があるみたいだ。
English: You must have quite a lot of respect for them.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんは、なんの疑問も 抱かずに、コイツに従っていた。
English: After all, you followed their instructions without question.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、次の問題だ。
English: So here's my next question.

Japanese: いったい‥‥指示書は、 だれが書いたものなのか‥‥？
English: Who wrote this set of instructions for you to follow...?

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんは、きっと 信頼しているはずだ。
English: This is the person who wrote the instructions, isn't it Pearls?

Japanese: この指示書を書いた人物のことを！
English: You must have had a lot of faith in them to follow their instructions to a tee!

Japanese: わたくし‥‥ I...
信じていました。 ‥‥なるほどくんのこと‥‥。
English: I had a lot of faith... in you, Mr. Nick.

Japanese: そ、それは‥‥ありがとう。
English: Oh! Thanks!

Japanese: でも‥‥‥
English: But...

Japanese: それも、今日かぎりですっ！
English: ...I don't think I can trust you anymore.

Japanese: （ううう‥‥きくなあ。 春美ちゃんのヒトコト‥‥）
English: (Ouch... Pearls sure can be blunt when she wants to be...)

Japanese: （春美ちゃんが、なんのギモンも 抱かず、信頼している人物‥‥）
English: (Alright now, think! Someone who Pearls would trust, no questions asked...)

Japanese: （それほど、多くはないはずだ！）
English: (There can't be many candidates for that post.)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: Pearls...

Japanese: きみは、きっと‥‥この指示書が 何をイミしているのか‥‥
English: I have to wonder about something...

Japanese: 理解していないんじゃないかな？
English: You didn't have any idea what these instructions meant, did you?

Japanese: ‥‥どきっ。
English: ...*ba-dump*

Japanese: それでも、《指示書》のとおりに 行動したのは‥‥
English: But you followed them to the letter regardless.

Japanese: それが、おかあさんからの 言いつけだったからだ！
English: Why? Because it was your own mother who asked you. That's why.

Japanese: ど、どうして‥‥それを！
English: H-How did you...?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。この《指示書》は‥‥
English: I figured it out.

Japanese: 春美ちゃんのおかあさん‥‥ 綾里　キミ子さんが書いた！
English: The person who wrote you this letter was your own mother, Morgan Fey!
Uh...

Japanese: うわあああああああああああんっ！
English: Waaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ‥‥どうかな、春美ちゃん。 そろそろ、本当のコトを‥‥
English: Alright, Pearls! It's time you started telling me the truth.

Japanese: な、な、な、な、なるほどくん あ、あ、あ、あ、あのっ！
English: M-M-Mr. N-Nick... I-I-I...

Japanese: （なんか、イジめてるみたいで イヤな感じだなあ、ぼく‥‥）
English: (Why's she holding back from me? I don't like this...)

Japanese: こ、こ、こ、こ、子どもだと思って あ、アマく見ないでくださいっ！
English: D-D-Don't underestimate m-me, j-just because I'm a ch-child!
Huh?

Japanese: わたくしが《指示書》のとおりに 行動したという‥‥
English: If you're trying to say I followed these instructions...

Japanese: ‥‥そんな証拠が、 あるのですかわわああああんっ！
English: I'd l-like to see some proof!

Japanese: えええええええッ！
English: Whaaaat!?

Japanese: わたくし‥‥そ、そのようなもの、 し、し、し、し、知りませんもの！
English: Because... I... I d-don't think y-y-you have a-a-any!

Japanese: うむむむむむむむむ‥‥‥
English: Nnnnnngh...

Japanese: （‥‥あくまでも、《指示書》の 言いつけを守るつもりか‥‥）
English: (She'd say anything rather than admit to carrying out those instructions...)

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥これが、 最後の証拠になりそうだな）
English: (I guess I'll have to produce some more evidence, then. One more thing should do it.)

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃん。
English: ...Alright, Pearls.

Japanese: 事件当夜‥‥だれかが、この 指示どおりのコトをしたんだ。
English: We both know someone carried out these instructions on the night of the murder.

Japanese: たしかに、それが春美ちゃんだった という証拠は、ない。
English: But you're right. There's no evidence that proves it was you.

Japanese: や‥‥‥‥やっぱり！ そ‥‥‥‥そうですよねっ！
English: I-I-I knew it!
However...

Japanese: この指示に従ったのは、 “子ども”だったんだよ。
English: I do know that whoever did it was a child.

Japanese: ど‥‥どうして、そんな！
English: Huh? H-How do you know that!?

Japanese: ‥‥もし、オトナだったのならば。
English: It couldn't have been an adult...

Japanese: こんな思いちがいは しなかったはずなんだよ。
English: No adult would've made a simple mistake like that.

Japanese: お‥‥“思いちがい”‥‥？
English: A... simple mistake? What do you mean by that?

Japanese: 必死で、書いてあることに 従おうとしたんだろうけど‥‥
English: I'm sure you thought you were carefully following the instructions you'd been given.

Japanese: 指示書のイミをとりちがえたんだ。 ‥‥それを示す証拠があるはずだ！
English: But you misunderstood some of the words, and this is the evidence that proves it!

Japanese: これが‥‥“思いちがい”だよ！
English: This shows the mistake you made!

Japanese: あの‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: もう言わなくても わかると思うのですが‥‥
English: I guess you know what I'm about to say, huh?

Japanese: 思いちがいをしているのは、 なるほどくんのほうだと思います。
English: I don't think it's me who made a mistake, Mr. Nick. It's you.

Japanese: （‥‥指示書に従った人物は、 まだ子どもだった‥‥）
English: (But the person who read these instructions must have been a child.)

Japanese: （だから‥‥あれが ちゃんと、読めなかった）
English: (That's why they misunderstood the meaning of that word...)

Japanese: （そして‥‥“思いちがい”を したんだ‥‥！）
English: (It's the only explanation for how such a simple mistake could've been made!)

Japanese: もう一度だ、春美ちゃん！ この指示書に従ったのが‥‥
English: OK, Pearls. I'll go over it again. The person who read these instructions...

Japanese: この掛け軸‥‥ 春美ちゃんがやったんだね？
English: It was you who splattered gravy on this hanging scroll, wasn't it?

Japanese: ええええっ！
English: Ah!

Japanese: あああの。わわ、わたくしが、 いいい、いったいナニを‥‥っ！
English: Wh-What!? Wh-Why would I d-do something like...?

Japanese: 《指示書》に、こう書かれている。
English: Do you remember what was written in that letter?

Japanese: 『家元に、華麗に引導を たたきつけてやりなさい』
English: "Gravely roast the Master in the fires of Hades and bring our vengeance to fruition."

Japanese: 《華麗》‥‥《引導》‥‥ この漢字が、読めなかったんだ。
English: But you didn't know how to read the words "gravely" and "roast" among others, right?

Japanese: ど‥‥ どうして、それを‥‥！
English: H-How do you know that!?

Japanese: ‥‥思い出したんだよ。 事件があった、あの晩‥‥
English: Remember the conversation you had with Ms. Deauxnim on the night of the murder...?

Japanese: いっしょに、 ご本を読んであげましょう。
English: Perhaps we can read some books together.

Japanese: えっ！　よ、よ、 よろしいのですか？
English: R-Really!? I'd love to!

Japanese: あ。あの、わたくし。 読めない漢字があるのですけれど。
English: I, umm... I'm not very good at reading.

Japanese: ふふふ。いっしょに お勉強しましょうか。
English: Ha ha ha. Well then, would you like to practice reading with me?

Japanese: あ、あの。先生！　たとえば、 これは、なんと読むのですか？
English: Um, Ms. Elise? So, for example, how do you read this?

Japanese: “華麗”‥‥ムズカシイ字。 これは、《かれい》ね。
English: It says, "gravely"... That's kind of a tough word.

Japanese: 《カレイ》《インドウ》 ‥‥読み方は、わかった。
English: Sure, Ms. Deauxnim taught you how to read "gravely" and "roast".

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: But what she didn't teach you...

Japanese: 今度は、そのコトバの イミがわからなかったんだ。
English: ...is what they meant!

Japanese: 《カレー》といえば《インド》 ‥‥わたくし、そう思ったのです。
English: "Gravely" sounded like "gravy" to me, and the only "roast" I could think of was the food...

Japanese: だから‥‥
English: And that's why you did it...

Japanese: たたきつけたわけだね？ インドのカレーを‥‥“家元”に。
English: That's why you covered the picture of the Master in gravy from that night's pot roast.

Japanese: 正直なところ‥‥
English: To be honest...

Japanese: 自分でも、ちょっとヘンだな、 とは思っておりました。
English: I did think it was a bit strange.

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり‥‥ ちがった、のですね。
English: I guess I really did get the wrong idea...

Japanese: ちょっと、ね。
English: Just a tiny bit.

Japanese: わたくし‥‥本当に、 役に立たないのですね。
English: I... I really am useless!

Japanese: おかあさまの指示も守れず‥‥
English: I didn't even manage to burn the letter properly as my mother had asked of me...

Japanese: お手紙を焼くことすら、 マンゾクにできないなんて‥‥
English: Such a simple thing... And I couldn't even do it right...

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　山門
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Gate

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、ビキニさんは もう本堂に戻っているかな。
English: I wonder if Sister Bikini has gone back to the Main Hall already.

Japanese: それに、さっきからイトノコ刑事の 姿が見あたらないけど‥‥
English: I haven't seen Gumshoe around for a while, either.

Japanese: なかよく、お茶でも 飲んでいるかもしれないな。
English: Maybe they're having a cup of tea together somewhere.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事、オバサンに 好かれるタイプみたいだから。
English: I bet Gumshoe is Sister Bikini's type.

Japanese: それにしても、でっかい 山門だなあ。
English: This is one impressive gate.

Japanese: 分家の霊場とはいえ、 リッパなものだ。
English: It's pretty grand for something that belongs to a branch family of the clan.

Japanese: ‥‥意外に、もうかっているの かもしれないな。
English: I bet they make an exorbitant amount of money here.

Japanese: 修行に《スペシャル・コース》 なんて、商売上手だし。
English: I mean, that whole Special Course thing has got to be good business!

Japanese: 発想が、真宵ちゃんみたいに なってきちまった。
English: Ack! Noooo!! I'm starting to think like Maya!

Japanese: ‥‥事件当夜、コイツは 何のために使用されたんだろう。
English: I wonder what this thing was used for on the night of the murder...

Japanese: １本しかないキーを持っていた のは、あやめさんだから‥‥
English: There is only one key to this snowmobile, and Iris is the one who had it.

Japanese: 使ったのは、彼女‥‥かな。 でも、あやめさん自身は‥‥
English: So I have to wonder if it was her...

Japanese: 葉桜院からは出ていない、 って言ってるんだよな‥‥。
English: ...But she says she never left Hazakura Temple that night.

Japanese: かわいらしい鐘つき堂だ。
English: It's a quaint, little bell tower.

Japanese: あやめさんが《消灯の鐘》を 鳴らしたときは‥‥
English: I never would've thought that something this horrible was about to happen...

Japanese: まさか、こんな大変なコトに なるなんて、思わなかったのに。
English: ...when Iris rang the lights out bell that night.

Japanese: ここから、 葉桜院の本堂が見える。
English: You can see the Main Hall from here.

Japanese: ビキニさんも、そろそろ 帰っているだろう。
English: Sister Bikini should be back by now.

Japanese: 地震が起きたとき、 話を中断されちまったけど‥‥
English: We were interrupted earlier by the earthquake.

Japanese: しっかり、聞き出しておいた ほうがよさそうだな。
English: I think I should go pick up our conversation where we left off.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: ビキニさん‥‥ いないみたいだ。
English: Looks like Sister Bikini is not here.

Japanese: 地震のあと始末だろうか。 コシを痛めてるのに、大変だな‥‥
English: She must be sorting things out after the quake. Must be hard on her with her bad back.

Japanese: ‥‥ビキニさんには、どうしても、 聞いておくべきことがある。
English: There are a few things I really need to discuss with her.

Japanese: あとで、また来てみよう。
English: Guess I'll drop by again later and see if she's around.

Japanese: 笑ってしまうほど巨大な勾玉が うっそり、まつられている。
English: There's a laughably large Magatama on the altar.

Japanese: こんなちっぽけなシロモノでも サイコ・ロックが見えるんだから、
English: If I can see Psyche-Locks with the tiny Magatama I've got...

Japanese: これだけ大きかったら、 何が見えてもフシギじゃないな。
English: ...I can only imagine what kind of power this bad boy has under his hood!

Japanese: 祭壇だ。古びているが、 キレイに手入れされている。
English: It's an altar. It looks a bit old, but someone does a good job taking care of it.

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば去年、 倉院の里でも見かけたな。
English: Speaking of altars, I remember seeing one like this in Kurain Village last year.

Japanese: やっぱり、霊力に 関係しているのだろうか。
English: I guess they really do have something to do with spiritual power after all.

Japanese: 読めないけど、まがまがしい 呪文がびっしり書かれている。
English: I bet the writing on these sliding doors are instructions for spirit channeling...

Japanese: 数学の公式をトイレに張って おぼえる、みたいなコトなのか、
English: Kinda like how we scribbled math formulas on the bathroom walls to remember them.

Japanese: それとも、一種のカンニング ペーパーみたいなものなのか‥‥
English: Except we couldn't go to the bathroom during tests... And I can't read these walls.

Japanese: 火鉢なんて、テレビの時代劇で しか見たことがなかったけど、
English: I've only seen a layout like this when I saw this really old Japanese movie on TV.

Japanese: 意外にあたたかいものだ。
English: Wow! This hibachi brazier really puts out a lot of heat!

Japanese: あの夜は、みんなでコレを囲んで、 汗だくでカレーをかきこんだのに。
English: Just thinking about how happy we were, sitting here eating pot roast and gravy... *sigh*

Japanese: ワラで編んだ 円座がつまれている。
English: It's a stack of straw zabuton cushions.

Japanese: ‥‥きっと、ここに来なかったら 一生、知らなかっただろうな。
English: Maya told me this type of zabuton is called "enza"...

Japanese: 《円座》なんてコトバ。
English: I suppose if I'd never come here, I'd have missed out on all these new things.

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　本堂
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Main Hall

Japanese: ‥‥で？　どうだったんだい、 修験洞は！
English: Well!? What's the damage!? How is the Sacred Cavern!?

Japanese: なんとか、ブジでした。 ‥‥でも、大きなモンダイが‥‥
English: It looks like it survived. But we have a bigger problem now.

Japanese: （ぼくは、ビキニさんに話した）
English: (I told Sister Bikini all about it...)

Japanese: （修験洞への進入を拒む、 ５つの《錠》のこと‥‥）
English: (About the five locks that were stopping us from getting inside the Sacred Cavern.)

Japanese: い‥‥いったい、だれが そんなことを‥‥
English: But who... who could've done that...?

Japanese: からくり錠を使えるのは、 倉院の人間だけなのに‥‥
English: The only people who know how to set those trick locks are those of the Kurain Tradition.

Japanese: 《錠》のことは、あやめさんに おねがいしました。
English: We have Iris at the Sacred Cavern, trying her best to open them for us right now.

Japanese: ビキニさんには‥‥ お話をうかがいたいんです。
English: Actually, there's something I've been meaning to talk with you about, Sister Bikini.

Japanese: ああ‥‥。さっきは 途中だったっけねえ。
English: Oh, yes. We were in the middle of a chat, weren't we?

Japanese: １７年間、姿を消していた 倉院流霊媒道の家元‥‥
English: Yes. You were telling me how the Master of Kurain disappeared 17 years ago.

Japanese: 綾里　舞子がなぜ、 この葉桜院に姿を見せたか？
English: I want to know why Misty Fey suddenly appeared at this temple.

Japanese: １週間前‥‥エリスさま、いえ。 ‥‥舞子さまが、いらっしゃった。
English: It was about a week ago when Mystic Elise... No, I mean Mystic Misty, arrived.

Japanese: 家元のアカシを示してから‥‥ あの方は、こうおっしゃったの。
English: After she showed me the talisman that proved she was the Master, she said...

Japanese: 《倉院流霊媒道・本家の血が 絶やされようとしている》
English: "Someone is trying to destroy the Kurain Tradition's main family line."

Japanese: 《私は、それを阻むために ここへ来た》‥‥って。
English: "I am here to put a stop to them."

Japanese: 本家の‥‥血‥‥？
English: Someone was trying to destroy the main family line?

Japanese: 倉院流の真の後継者は、一子相伝 ‥‥たったひとりきり、なの。
English: There is only one heir to the title of "Master" at any time, and it's usually the eldest.

Japanese: その血を継ぐ霊媒師‥‥ 家元と、その娘が《本家》よ。
English: That child becomes the new Master of Kurain, and her daughters, the main family.

Japanese: それ以外の霊媒師は《分家》と 呼ばれ、決して頂点には立てない。
English: All other mediums become branch family members, with no hope of controlling the clan.

Japanese: だから‥‥倉院流の歴史では、 何度もくりかえされてきたわ。
English: That's why nothing has changed throughout the history of the clan...

Japanese: 《分家》が《本家》を根絶やしに しようとする、暗殺計画が‥‥
English: Branch families always have and always will plot to erase those of the main family line.

Japanese: （そ、そんな重たいものなのか ‥‥《家元》って‥‥）
English: (Is the power of the Master worth this much bloodshed...?)

Japanese: ありがとうございました。
English: Thank you very much.

Japanese: おかげで、いろいろなことが わかってきました。
English: You've helped clear up a lot of details.

Japanese: ‥‥春美ちゃんに‥‥ ２人も、お姉さんがいたなんて。
English: Pearls had two older sisters, huh...

Japanese: きっと、それは‥‥
English: Yes, that's right...

Japanese: キミ子さまの《本家》に 対する、執念なんだねえ‥‥
English: No doubt it was all because of Mystic Morgan's anger towards the main family...

Japanese: しゅうねん‥‥？
English: Anger? What do you mean?

Japanese: 双子の娘たちを、宝石商の だんなさまに奪われて‥‥
English: Her twin daughters were taken from her by her jeweler husband.

Japanese: それでも、娘を《家元》にする 夢を、あきらめきれなかった‥‥
English: But even that couldn't destroy her dream that a child of hers would one day lead the clan.

Japanese: （だから‥‥春美ちゃんが 生まれたのか‥‥）
English: (That's why she had Pearls?)

Japanese: 今度の事件も‥‥
English: It seems that the incident here was a result of Mystic Morgan's anger.

Japanese: 独房にいるキミ子さまの執念が 引き起こしたのかもしれないね‥‥
English: It was able to break free of the bars that confine her.

Japanese: （‥‥あやめさんの 双子のお姉さん‥‥か）
English: (So Iris has a twin sister...)

Japanese: （とにかく‥‥）
English: (The plot thickens...)

Japanese: （こんなアイマイな姿のままでは、 なんの武器にもならない！）
English: (But this information will be useless unless I can flesh it out a bit.)

Japanese: （‥‥なんとかして、もっと 《情報》を集めるんだ‥‥！）
English: (I need to ask more questions, and get some more info!)

Japanese: アンタも、知ってるんでしょ？ 倉院流が“本物”だ、って‥‥
English: You believe in the Technique, don't you Mr. Wright? You know its power is real...

Japanese: え‥‥ええ。
English: Yes... I do...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんと会ってから、 この３年間‥‥）
English: (It's been three years since I first met Maya.)

Japanese: （ぼくは、何度も見てきた。 彼女に、死者が宿るのを‥‥）
English: (In that time, I've seen her channel the spirits of the dead plenty of times.)

Japanese: （仮面マスクの事件でも‥‥ マコちゃんの事件でも‥‥）
English: (During the Mask☆DeMasque case, and during Maggey Byrde's trial, too.)

Japanese: （千尋さんは、いつでも ぼくを助けてくれた）
English: (Thanks to Maya, Mia's always on hand to help me out when I need her the most.)

Japanese: 倉院流は、この世界に対して、 大きな影響力を持ってきたわ。
English: The Kurain Technique has made a huge difference to the world, you know.

Japanese: 当然よね。死者と語らう、 本当の霊能力なんだから。
English: I mean, the ability to commune with the dead... That's true psychic power, you know.

Japanese: その時代の権力者たちの影に、 かならず倉院流が存在した。
English: Members of the Tradition have always been there, behind every important leader.

Japanese: その権威は、ゼッタイで‥‥ だれもが敬意を払っていたの。
English: Who wouldn't respect such a tremendous skill?
I suppose so, but...

Japanese: そのわりには、あまり‥‥その。 生活が豊かであるようには‥‥
English: With all that power and influence, I kind of expected you'd be really rich by now...

Japanese: ビンボーくさい、って？ わはは。ハッキリ言うわね。
English: Are you saying we're poor, Mr. Wright? Ho ho ho! You're very direct, aren't you?

Japanese: ‥‥信用を失ったのよ。 たった一度の《失敗》で‥‥
English: People don't believe in it anymore, you see. All because of one little mistake...
A mistake? What mistake?

Japanese: 今から、１７年前のことよ。 倉院流の権威が、地に落ちたわ。
English: It was 17 years ago. That's when everything came crashing down.

Japanese: ある事件によって、ね。
English: All because of that incident.

Japanese: （その《事件》って‥‥ も、もしかして‥‥？）
English: ("That incident"? Is she talking about what I think she is...?)

Japanese: 今でも、記録が残っているはずよ。 ‥‥もちろん、封印されてるけど。
English: I'm sure the records still exist if you're interested. Locked up, of course.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》‥‥ それが、記録に与えられた名前よ。
English: It was called the "DL-6 Incident". That's the name of the file.

Japanese: 《ＤＬ６号事件》‥‥ （やっぱり‥‥そうだったか！）
English: The DL-6 Incident, huh?

Japanese: ＤＬ６号事件‥‥
English: The DL-6 Incident...

Japanese: その事件なら‥‥ よく、知っています。
English: ...I know it well.

Japanese: 今から、３年前‥‥ ぼくが扱った事件、ですから。
English: I handled a related case just three years ago.

Japanese: そう‥‥。あの弁護士‥‥ アンタ、だったのかい‥‥
English: Ah! That's right... So it was you who was the defense attorney on that case...

Japanese: （１７年前に起こった、 ある殺人事件‥‥）
English: (A murder that took place 17 years ago...)

Japanese: ‥‥この国の警察の歴史で初めて、 霊媒師に協力が求められたの。
English: It was the first time in the country's history the police ever used a spirit medium.

Japanese: 被害者の霊を呼び出して、 犯人の名を聞く‥‥という試みね。
English: The idea was to channel the victim's spirit to learn the identity of the murderer.

Japanese: （その霊媒師が、綾里　舞子‥‥ 真宵ちゃんのおかあさんだった）
English: (And the medium who performed the channeling was Misty Fey, Maya's mother.)

Japanese: ‥‥舞子さまの霊媒によって、 １人の男の名が浮かび上がったわ。
English: Through Mystic Misty's channeling, the name of a certain man surfaced.

Japanese: 捜査陣は、色めきたった。 犯人の名が、わかったのだから‥‥
English: Armed with that as evidence, the investigators were spurred into action...

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 でも、その《男》は‥‥
English: But that man...

Japanese: 裁判の結果‥‥‥無罪判決が 下されてしまった。
English: He was found not guilty, wasn't he?

Japanese: ‥‥そのとおりよ。
English: ...That's right. He was.

Japanese: そして‥‥、事件はそのまま 迷宮入りしてしまったわ。
English: And the case remained a mystery...

Japanese: 私たちは、失敗したの。
English: In other words, we failed.

Japanese: 倉院流霊媒道が、初めて 一般世間に姿を見せた事件で‥‥
English: It was the first case the world had ever seen the Kurain Tradition openly involved in.

Japanese: マスコミ、司法当局、世論、 そして‥‥倉院の里の霊媒師たち。
English: It was all over the media. The public, the judiciary, the people of Kurain Village...

Japanese: あらゆるメディアが、舞子さまの チカラを“インチキ”だ、と‥‥。
English: Everyone judged her. Everyone said Mystic Misty's powers were a sham.

Japanese: そして‥‥あの方は失意のまま、 姿を消してしまった。
English: And then she just disappeared; vanished, while everyone still thought of her as a fraud.

Japanese: でも！　ぼくは、 その真相を知っています！
English: But I know the truth!

Japanese: 綾里　舞子さんの霊媒は‥‥ インチキではなかった！
English: Misty Fey's spirit channeling wasn't a sham at all!

Japanese: ‥‥もちろん、そうよ。
English: Of course it wasn't...

Japanese: あなたのおかげで、 事件の真相がわかってから‥‥
English: And since you managed to reveal the truth, we're finally making a comeback.

Japanese: 倉院は、やっと‥‥ふたたび、 チカラを取り戻そうとしているの。
English: The Kurain Tradition is starting to recover at last.

Japanese: その《チカラ》を背負って立つのが ‥‥新しい家元さま、なのよ。
English: But with a new Master wielding the power of the clan...

Japanese: （それが‥‥‥ 真宵ちゃん‥‥なのか！）
English: (Does she mean Maya!?)

Japanese: 倉院流の霊力は、 その血に宿っているわ。
English: The spiritual power of the Kurain Channeling Technique is in the blood.

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんも‥‥ そう言っていたっけ）
English: (Maya's told me the exact same thing before...)

Japanese: あたしたち綾里家の女は、代々 霊媒師として生きてきたの。
English: We, the women of the Fey family, have always been spirit mediums.

Japanese: 綾里の血は、強い霊力を 宿しているから‥‥。
English: It's because the power to communicate with spirits flows strongly through us.

Japanese: （霊力は‥‥綾里家の女性の 血にのみ、受け継がれるらしい）
English: (According to Maya, only the women in the family can inherit that power.)

Japanese: 綾里　供子さまの血を受けた 《家元》の娘たちの中から‥‥
English: The main family's bloodline stems directly from Mystic Ami.

Japanese: 代々、たったひとり‥‥ 次の《家元》が決められるの。
English: But with each new generation, only one daughter becomes the new Master.

Japanese: 選ばれなかった娘さんは、 《分家》になるわけですね。
English: And the ones who don't become branch families, right?

Japanese: ‥‥そうよ。それが、 いくつもの悲劇を生んできたわ。
English: That's right. And it's always the cause of tragedy.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんには、お姉さんが いたんです。千尋さん、という‥‥
English: You know, Maya had a sister, too. An older sister named Mia...

Japanese: 聞いたこと、あるわ‥‥ お姉さまのこと。
English: Oh yes. I've heard of her.

Japanese: ‥‥‥！　知ってるんですか？
English: What!? You know about Mia!?

Japanese: おかあさま‥‥舞子さまの事件を 調べるため、弁護士になったとか。
English: Of course. She became a lawyer in the hopes of discovering what happened to her mother.

Japanese: （そして‥‥その事件を追ううちに 命を落としてしまった‥‥）
English: (And lost her life as a result...)

Japanese: ‥‥千尋さまは、 おっしゃっていたそうよ。
English: Do you know what Mystic Mia is rumored to have said?

Japanese: 自分が弁護士になったのは、 おかあさまのためだけじゃない‥‥
English: She said it wasn't only because of her mother that she became a lawyer...

Japanese: 姉妹で、家元の座をめぐって 争いたくないからだ、って。
English: She also didn't want to fight with her sister over the leadership of the Tradition.

Japanese: そ‥‥そうなんですか‥‥！
English: Really...!?

Japanese: あの方は、おかあさんの 舞子さまを見て育ったからねえ。
English: Well, she saw what happened to her own mother, Mystic Misty, as she grew up.

Japanese: きっと、姉妹のミニクイ争いに イヤ気がさしていたんだろうねえ。
English: I guess Mystic Mia got tired of seeing all the rivalry between her mom and her aunt.

Japanese: （綾里　舞子さんには‥‥ ひとり、お姉さんがいた）
English: (That's right. Misty Fey had an elder sister, too.)

Japanese: （舞子さんの強い霊力によって 《家元》の座を追われた‥‥）
English: (And Misty, having superior powers, managed to usurp the Master's seat from her...)

Japanese: ‥‥綾里　キミ子さま、だね。
English: Mystic Misty's sister is Mystic Morgan, as you probably know.

Japanese: （綾里　キミ子‥‥その名前を、 ぼくは、よく知っている）
English: Morgan? There's a name I know well.

Japanese: （今から１年前。真宵ちゃんの ふるさと《倉院の里》で‥‥）
English: It was a year ago now, at Maya's home, Kurain Village.

Japanese: （自分の娘を家元にするため、 恐ろしい事件を起こした‥‥）
English: What she did was terrible. It was all so she could make her own daughter the next Master.

Japanese: キミ子さまの娘さんを知っている ‥‥ということは‥‥
English: I suppose if you know about Mystic Morgan's daughter...

Japanese: アンタ‥‥気づいていたのかい？ ‥‥あやめのこと‥‥
English: ...then you must've already realized that Iris...

Japanese: え？　あ‥‥あやめさん‥‥？ （どうして、彼女の名前が‥‥）
English: Huh? Iris...? (What's Iris got to do with any of this?)

Japanese: あやめは‥‥あの子は‥‥‥ キミ子さまの娘なんだよ。
English: That Iris is Mystic Morgan's daughter...
Wh...

Japanese: なんですってェェッ！ あ、あやめさんが‥‥？
English: IRIS IS WHAT!?

Japanese: （あの、あやめさんが‥‥ 綾里　キミ子の娘、だって！）
English: (Is she kidding me!? Iris is Morgan Fey's daughter!?)

Japanese: あ‥‥あやめさんが、 綾里　キミ子の、娘‥‥？
English: Did you just say that Iris is Morgan Fey's daughter?

Japanese: おや。知らなかったのかい？
English: Oops! I thought you already knew!

Japanese: キミ子さまのおじょうさまを 知ってるみたいだったから‥‥
English: It sounded like you'd met one of Mystic Morgan's daughters already.

Japanese: え、ええ。たしかに知っています。 キミ子さんの娘さんなら‥‥
English: I-I have. I know her very well.

Japanese: どういうコトだい？
English: Oh?

Japanese: 春美ちゃん‥‥ですよ。
English: Yes. Pearls. Pearl Fey.

Japanese: あの子が、キミ子さんのヒトリ娘。 ‥‥ずっと、そう思っていました。
English: But I always thought she was an only child.

Japanese: ええッ！　あの子が、 キミ子さまの‥‥
English: You jest! That child... She's Mystic Morgan's...

Japanese: 知らなかった、わ‥‥
English: I had no idea...

Japanese: ‥‥キミ子さま‥‥
English: ...Mystic Morgan...

Japanese: キミ子さまは、今‥‥ 刑務所にいらっしゃるのよね？
English: But she's in prison now, isn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥ええ。 １年前の、あの殺人事件‥‥
English: Yes. Ever since she was found to be a co-conspirator in a murder case last year.

Japanese: すべては‥‥、春美ちゃんを 《家元》にするためだったんです。
English: It was all done to set Pearls up as the next Master.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのかい。 ということは、キミ子さまには‥‥
English: I see. So I've been wrong all this time.

Japanese: 娘さんが３人、いたんだねえ‥‥
English: Mystic Morgan had three daughters, not two.
Whaaa!? ...Th-Three?

Japanese: ‥‥あやめには、 双子の姉がいるんだよ。
English: Yes. Iris, her twin sister, and Mystic Pearl.

Japanese: なんですって‥‥！ ふ‥‥ふたご‥‥？
English: WHAT!? Tw-Twin sister?

Japanese: 今から、２０年前‥‥
English: Oh, didn't you know? It all happened twenty years ago...

Japanese: 家元の座を妹に奪われたとき、 キミ子さまの家庭は、崩壊したわ。
English: After the clan leadership was taken from her by her sister, Mystic Morgan's life crumbled.

Japanese: ‥‥その数年後、 倉院の権威は地に落ちた。
English: It wasn't many years later that Kurain's reputation hit an all-time low.

Japanese: 妻が家元になれないと知った キミ子さまの夫は‥‥
English: When Mystic Morgan's husband realized his wife would never become the Master...

Japanese: おさない双子の娘たちを連れて、 倉院の里を去ったの。
English: ...he left her and the village, taking their twin daughters with him.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったんですか‥‥
English: How awful...

Japanese: だんなさまは、宝石商だった。 やがて、再婚した際に‥‥
English: He was a jeweler, you know. In the end he remarried, and that's when it happened.

Japanese: 双子のうちのひとりを、 この葉桜院にあずけたの。
English: He decided to give one of his girls up, to be looked after by us here at the temple.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、あやめよ。
English: That was Iris, you see.

Japanese: （あやめさんに‥‥ 双子のお姉さんが‥‥！）
English: (It's unbelievable! If Iris has a twin sister...)

Japanese: （‥‥その、“お姉さん”‥‥ もしかして‥‥）
English: (Could it be...?)

Japanese: あ、あの‥‥１つだけ 教えてください！
English: Um, could you tell me one more thing, Sister Bikini?

Japanese: あやめさんの‥‥ 双子のお姉さんの、名前は‥‥？
English: What was the name of Iris's sister?

Japanese: ごめんなさい。
English: I'm sorry, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥おぼえてないわ。 父親が宝石商、としか‥‥
English: I just can't remember. All I can recall is that her father was a jeweler.

Japanese: （宝石商、か‥‥）
English: (Well, that's a clue, I guess. Hmm... A jeweler...)

Japanese: オバサンのせいなのよねぇ‥‥ おもに、オバサンのコシのせい。
English: It's all my fault, isn't it? It's all my achy back's fault.

Japanese: エリスさまが亡くなって、 修験者さまも閉じこめられて‥‥
English: Now Mystic Elise is dead, and an acolyte is trapped inside the Inner Temple...

Japanese: 小さな女の子も愛想をつかして、 どこか行っちゃって。
English: ...and that poor little girl has gone missing, too.

Japanese: （“どこか”ですむ モンダイじゃないけど‥‥）
English: ("Missing"...? I hope that's all it is...)

Japanese: ‥‥まだまだ。オバサン、 修行が足りないのねえ‥‥。
English: Oh dear, oh dear. I've got to train to withstand more pain, don't I...?

Japanese: ‥‥そうだったのかい。 この方が‥‥新しい家元さま‥‥
English: So... This young lady is the new Master...

Japanese: そうです。綾里　真宵。
English: That's right. Maya Fey.

Japanese: ‥‥そうと知ってたら‥‥
English: Oh dear, oh dear... If I'd known who she was...

Japanese: カレーなんて手を抜かないで、 ゴチソウを出せばよかったねえ。
English: I would've prepared a feast, not a boring old pot roast and some gravy!

Japanese: 彼女を助けたら、イヤというほど ゴチソウしてあげてください。
English: Once we get her out, you can give her all the feasts you want.

Japanese: モチロンよ！　アンタ！ ‥‥よろしくおねがいしますよ。
English: Of course! You just make sure you get her out, alright?

Japanese: 家元さまを、助けてあげて‥‥
English: She's the Master! You just have to rescue her!

Japanese: ああ‥‥この子。 かわいそうにねェ‥‥
English: Ah... This poor little girl...

Japanese: （真宵ちゃんにつづいて、 春美ちゃんまで‥‥）
English: (First I lost Maya, and now Pearls...)

Japanese: （どこまでぼくを 苦しめるんだ、この事件は！）
English: (How much more pain is this case going to cause me!?)

Japanese: （‥‥ううううう。 熱で、アタマもいたいし‥‥）
English: (Urgh... Not to mention this fever is giving me a killer migraine...)

Japanese: オバサン‥‥どうやって おわびをしたらいいのか‥‥
English: How... How can I ever make up for this...?

Japanese: おわび‥‥ですか？
English: Make up for what?

Japanese: ‥‥あんなダイジな方を 死なせてしまうなんて‥‥！
English: For allowing harm to come to such an important person!

Japanese: （倉院流の家元だった わけだからな、エリス先生‥‥）
English: (Sister Bikini must feel really responsible for what happened...)

Japanese: （セキニンを感じるのも ムリはない、か‥‥）
English: (Ms. Deauxnim was the Master after all...)

Japanese: ね。ね。アンタ！ どーなのよ、あやめは！
English: Please, tell me! What's going to happen to poor Iris!?

Japanese: ユーザイにしたら、もお‥‥
English: If she was to be found guilty, I'd...

Japanese: ショウチしないからねッ！
English: Well, I wouldn't accept it!

Japanese: それは‥‥ビキニさんの 証言しだい、だったのでは‥‥
English: But your testimony, Sister Bikini...

Japanese: しょうがないでしょ！ 見ちゃったんだから！
English: I can't help my testimony! I saw what I saw!

Japanese: それをなんとかするのが、 プロってモンでしょうが！
English: It's up to a pro lawyer like you to deal with problems like that!

Japanese: まさか、あんな どうしようもない子だとはね！
English: What a disgraceful young man!

Japanese: ‥‥やっと わかってもらえましたか。
English: You've finally seen through him, huh...

Japanese: あんな、ウソの絵まで描いて！ “目立とう精神”ってヤツかねェ！
English: How could he sit there and claim he really saw what he drew in that picture?

Japanese: まあ、オバサンの似顔絵は なかなか上手だったケド。
English: Although, the portrait he did of me was rather flattering, I must say.
(Still...)

Japanese: （アイツは、女の子をヒドい目に あわせるウソはつかない）
English: (It's not like Larry to make something up that might get a woman he likes in trouble.)

Japanese: （‥‥きっと、このスケッチには イミがあると思うんだけど‥‥）
English: (There's more to that sketch of his than meets the eye. I'm sure of it.)

Japanese: まったく、アンタの友だちは ヘンなのばっかりだね！
English: You certainly have some unusual friends, don't you, Mr. Wright! Ho ho ho!

Japanese: と、ともだち‥‥
English: F-Friends...?

Japanese: あのニヤけた絵描きはともかく、 裁判長をたたくのはマズいでしょ。
English: That fancy-pansy painter is one thing, but whipping the judge is just bad form.

Japanese: ‥‥あの、それ‥‥
English: Well... Um...

Japanese: 本人に、よおく 言って聞かせてもらえませんか。
English: Do you think you could go drill that into her head for me...? Please?

Japanese: いやー、いいセンパイを持って シアワセねぇ、アンタ。
English: You must be very happy to have such a fine mentor.

Japanese: ‥‥センパイ？ コイツ、友だちですけど‥‥
English: Mentor...? No, he's just a friend...

Japanese: あらあら。アンタったら。 またまた。ジョーダンばっかり。
English: Oh, you naughty devil! Always the joker, aren't you? Ho ho ho ho!

Japanese: どう見ても、たよりになりそう だったよ、こっちのほうが。
English: Ah, he seemed like a lovely young man... Like the kind you can always count on.

Japanese: そそ、そうですか‥‥ （うう‥‥ちょっとだけ、悲しい）
English: Y-Yeah... (It's kinda depressing how much faith she has in him...)

Japanese: ‥‥考えてみれば、 かわいそうな方なんだよ。
English: When I think about it, I do kind of feel sorry for Mystic Morgan.

Japanese: 霊力の遺伝は、キホン的に 姉のほうが強いのに‥‥
English: It wasn't her fault that her sister was a more powerful spirit medium.

Japanese: ‥‥キミ子さまの霊力は、 妹の舞子さまにおよばなかった。
English: Mystic Morgan just couldn't compete with her sister.

Japanese: きっと‥‥ツラい思いを しただろうねえ‥‥
English: It must have been really tough on her...

Japanese: でも‥‥
English: Even so...

Japanese: 彼女のやったことは、ユルされる ことではありません。
English: That doesn't excuse all the things she did.

Japanese: そう、なんだよねえ‥‥
English: No... Of course not.

Japanese: あやめには、双子の お姉さんがいた‥‥
English: Iris had a twin sister...

Japanese: オバサンが知ってるのは、 それだけ‥‥だねえ。
English: That's really all I know about her.

Japanese: 宝石商のお父さんに 引き取られたらしいけど‥‥
English: That and the fact that she was taken away from her mother by her jeweler father.

Japanese: どこで、何をしているやら。
English: I don't have any idea where she is or what she's doing now.

Japanese: （それを知ったら‥‥）
English: (I could tell her...)

Japanese: （ビキニさん、 コシを抜かすな‥‥きっと）
English: (But she'd probably have a coronary and a half...)

Japanese: あの‥‥。この写真ですけど‥‥
English: Um, about this picture...

Japanese: ああ、これねえ‥‥。
English: Oh, that old thing!

Japanese: いつもなら、断っちゃうのよ。 取材なんて。
English: I always turned those reporter-types away in the past.

Japanese: 今回は、あやめが“どうしても” って言うモンだから。
English: But Iris was so insistent this time.

Japanese: あやめさんが‥‥？
English: Iris?

Japanese: ホラ。あの子、意外に 目立ちたがり屋さんだから。
English: Oh, yes! Iris loves to be the center of attention, you know.

Japanese: （それは、あまりに 意外すぎるぞ‥‥）
English: (Really? I find that kinda hard to believe...)

Japanese: まあまあまあまあ！ 《退魔のずきん》じゃない。
English: Well, well, well! That's a Demon-Warding Hood!

Japanese: 修験者さまは、悪霊に ねらわれやすいから。
English: Acolytes are highly susceptible to possession by evil spirits, you know.

Japanese: オバサンたち、いつも これをかぶっているワケよぉ。
English: That's why we always wear these for protection.

Japanese: 事件当夜、あやめさんに もらったんです。
English: Iris gave this to me on the night of the murder.

Japanese: ‥‥あれから、川に落ちて‥‥
English: But then I went and fell into the river and got this nasty fever...

Japanese: ずっと、これをかぶってたら、 早く熱が下がった気がしました！
English: I thought maybe if I were to wear this hood, it'd make my fever go down faster!

Japanese: 気のせいでしょ。 カゼと霊力は、カンケイないし。
English: I don't think so. Colds and spirit power have nothing to do with each other.

Japanese: （ユメがないなあ‥‥）
English: (Way to crush a man's hope.)

Japanese: オバサンの、目のサッカク だったのかねえ。
English: Maybe my eyes were playing tricks on me.

Japanese: あやめが、あんなオソロシイ ことをするなんて‥‥
English: I mean, Iris could never do such a wicked thing.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、 立証されたんですよね。
English: But they proved it in court today, didn't they?

Japanese: このカタナが凶器ではない、って。
English: That this sword wasn't the murder weapon.

Japanese: そうみたいだねえ‥‥。
English: That's what they were saying, yes.

Japanese: （でも‥‥このカタナは、 ぼくも目撃している‥‥）
English: (The problem is, I saw it, too...)

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥エリスさんは、 このカタナに刺されたはずだ）
English: (I'm sure that what I saw was this blade stabbed into Ms. Deauxnim's body.)

Japanese: エリスさまのツエ、だね。
English: Ah, it's Mystic Elise's staff.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥水晶が なくなってるよ。
English: Hmm... The crystal sphere really is missing from it.

Japanese: 水晶は、おぼろ橋の 近くで見つかったんですよね。
English: But the sphere was found near Dusky Bridge, right?

Japanese: ‥‥エリスさま、あの晩は 葉桜院にいたはずなのにねえ。
English: Yes. Even though I'm sure Mystic Elise was in Hazakura Temple that entire evening.

Japanese: この写真ですけど‥‥。
English: About this photograph...

Japanese: ‥‥まちがいなく、ウチの スノーモービルの跡だね。
English: Oh, yes. Those are our snowmobile's tracks, alright. I'd know them anywhere!

Japanese: （１本しか跡がないんだから‥‥）
English: (But there's only one set... Which means...)

Japanese: （雪が降っているあいだに 出かけて、やんでから戻った）
English: (...the rider went out while it was still snowing, and came back after it had stopped.)

Japanese: （そう考えるしか、ないよなあ）
English: (That's really the only logical explanation, I suppose.)

Japanese: ‥‥ホント、世も末だねえ。
English: Honestly! It's like the end of the world.

Japanese: まったくですね。
English: It's not a good thing, that's for sure.

Japanese: ‥‥こんなヘタな絵で、 ゲージュツカ、なんて。
English: To think people have the gall to call this kind of scribbling by the name of art!

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: こんなんでいいなら、オバサンも 今日から歌姫だよ！
English: If that's the sort of standard you need for the arts these days, I could be a pop diva!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。描いてある 内容のほうは‥‥？
English: Um, sure. But what about the contents of the picture? What do you make of that?

Japanese: ‥‥やっぱり、世も末だねえ。
English: It's definitely the end of the world.

Japanese: この水晶‥‥
English: This crystal sphere...

Japanese: 《退魔のずきん》のものじゃない。 ちょっと、似てるけど。
English: It's not from a Demon-Warding Hood, but I would guess it serves a similar purpose.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥エリス先生のツエに ついていたもの、ですか。
English: If it's not from a hood, then I guess it really is from Ms. Deauxnim's staff.

Japanese: 血ィがついてるのよねえ。 かわいそうなエリスさま‥‥
English: Look at the blood on it. Oh, poor Mystic Elise...

Japanese: あの。いかがでしょうか‥‥
English: I was wondering about this...

Japanese: ‥‥そおねえ。 どうかしらねえ‥‥
English: Hmm... Let me see...

Japanese: ホラ。オバサン、アレだから。 おじょーさまだからねェ。
English: Well, as you may know... In order to see reality for what it truly is...

Japanese: 世間知らずのモノ知らず、 恥知らずで親知らずなワケでしょ。
English: ...we strive to break our attachments to much of the transient, material realm.

Japanese: “ハコ入りムスメ”って言うの？
English: I guess you could call me an "immaterial girl"!

Japanese: （きっと、小さなハコにギュウと 詰めこまれてたんだろうな）
English: (I'm guessing she makes an exception for fine dining...)

Japanese: あの、ビキニさん。 ちょっと、これを見て
English: Um, would you mind taking a look at this, Sister Bikini?

Japanese: ぎゃあああああ！
English: Aaaah!

Japanese: ダイジな掛け軸に、 なな、なんてコトするの！
English: Our scroll! Our precious scroll! What on earth have you done to it!?

Japanese: いやいや！ ぼくじゃありませんよ！
English: No, no! It wasn't me! I swear!

Japanese: （‥‥この家元の掛け軸が、 なぜカレーまみれなのか‥‥）
English: (But who did this, and why would anyone want to cover this thing in gravy?)

Japanese: （事件にカンケイ あるのか‥‥？）
English: (More importantly, does this have anything to do with the case...?)

Japanese: あの、ビキニさん。 ちょっと、これを見て
English: Um, Sister Bikini. Would you mind taking a look at this?

Japanese: ぎゃあああああああああああッ！
English: Aaaaah!

Japanese: なな、なんでアナタがそれをッ！
English: What are you doing with that!?

Japanese: 早く返しなさいッ！
English: Give it back at once!

Japanese: ダメですよ！ 証拠なんですから。
English: I can't! This is evidence.

Japanese: （やはり‥‥これは 重要なものらしいな‥‥）
English: (This really must be one very important item...)

Japanese: 同日　某時刻 葉桜院　境内
English: February 9 Hazakura Temple Courtyard

Japanese: （‥‥風に乗って、どこからか 陽気な鼻歌が聞こえてくる）
English: (Sounds like someone's happy. What tune is that they're humming...?)

Japanese: ‥‥波のォォォ‥‥間に間にィィィ 証ォ拠がァ‥‥ハネりゃぁぁぁ‥‥
English: ♪No motive, no crime. No motive, no crime. Sing it with me - No motive, no crime!♪

Japanese: オレのォォォ‥‥ココロもォォォ ハネるゥのさぁぁぁ‥‥
English: ♪...I remember, when we use to search... in the Channeling Room in Kurain...♪

Japanese: いやー、捜査は楽しいッス。
English: Whoop! I love my job!

Japanese: ロクな手がかりが見つからないけど ‥‥そんなの、カンケイないッス！
English: Who cares if the clues I find are no good?

Japanese: 自分は、そんなもののために 捜査をしてるワケじゃないッス！
English: That's not what investigating's all about!

Japanese: 捜査が好きだから。オマエが 好きだから！　捜査をするッス！
English: The investigator investigates for the love of investigating! It's a passion!

Japanese: ‥‥夢のォォォ‥‥間に間にィィィ 証ォ拠がァ‥‥オドりゃぁぁぁ‥‥
English: ♪Good cases we have, good cases we've lost... Along the way...♪

Japanese: （‥‥セリフにつづいて ２番が始まったみたいだ）
English: (...I would have never guessed that Gumshoe was into reggae.)

Japanese: オレのォォォ‥‥ココロもォォォ
English: ♪Every case is gonna be air tight... Every case is gonna be air tight...♪

Japanese: アンタ。
English: Hey, pal.

Japanese: え。ぼくですか。
English: Huh? Who, me?

Japanese: いつから、そこにいるッス？
English: How long have you been there?

Japanese: 今、来たところですけど。
English: I just got here.

Japanese: ‥‥ホッ。
English: ...Oh, OK.

Japanese: 何してるんですか？ こんなところで。
English: So what are you up to, Detective?

Japanese: 捜査ッスよ、捜査。
English: I'm investigating, pal.

Japanese: 自分は、御剣検事と ヤクソクしたッス！
English: I made a promise to Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: 『かならず、本物のキョーキを 見つけだすッス』‥‥と！
English: I promised I would find the real murder weapon.
(The real murder weapon...)

Japanese: あの、ギザギザのカタナは ニセモノだったワケッスからね。
English: That barbed sword thing turned out to be a false lead, right?

Japanese: かなり、がんばってるのに 手がかりなしッス。
English: I'm giving it my best shot here, but I still haven't turned up any clues.

Japanese: ワケのわからない怪文書を 拾ったッスけど‥‥
English: I just found these weird scraps of paper. It looks like a letter or something.

Japanese: 事件にはカンケイ なさそうッスしねえ‥‥
English: It doesn't seem to have anything to do with the case, though...

Japanese: （かいぶんしょ‥‥？）
English: (A letter?)

Japanese: じゃ、自分はひとっぱしり、 鑑識課へ行ってくるッス！
English: OK, I'm gonna run over to forensics.

Japanese: たとえ血をふき取っても、 痕跡は残っているハズッスからね！
English: There's gotta be some traces of blood left, even if most of it has been wiped off.

Japanese: ‥‥じゃっ！
English: See you later, pal!

Japanese: ‥‥雪のォォォ‥‥間に間にィィィ 探ン知機ィ‥‥サワぎゃぁぁぁ‥‥
English: ♪Every case is gonna be air tight... Every case is gonna be air tight...♪

Japanese: ‥‥ま、待ってください！ イトノコ刑事！
English: Wait up, Detective!

Japanese: なんス？　自分は今、 かなり多忙な毎日ッス。
English: Huh? What is it, pal? I'm a pretty busy guy right now, you know.

Japanese: その仕込みヅエを調べるの‥‥
English: You're going to get that staff analyzed, right?

Japanese: ちょっとだけ、 待ってもらえませんか！
English: Would you mind holding off for just a while?

Japanese: なな、ナニをバカなこと 言ってるッスか！
English: Huh? What are you talking about, pal!?

Japanese: おねがいします！
English: Please!

Japanese: せめて‥‥真宵ちゃんが 見つかるまで‥‥
English: Just until we find Maya...

Japanese: 綾里　真宵‥‥ッスか‥‥？
English: Maya? What's this got to do with her?

Japanese: なぜか‥‥イヤなことを 考えてしまったんです。
English: I don't know, but I'm starting to get a really bad feeling in the pit of my stomach.

Japanese: ‥‥なんスか？ “イヤなこと”って‥‥
English: Oh? How so?

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんは‥‥今、修験洞に 閉じこめられています。
English: Look, Maya's trapped inside the Sacred Cavern right now and...

Japanese: でも‥‥本当にブジなのか‥‥
English: Well, we don't even know if she's OK or not...

Japanese: 証人たちの話を聞けば聞くほど、 不安になってきてしまって‥‥
English: The more evidence and testimony I hear, the more uneasy I feel.

Japanese: ま、まさか‥‥
English: Y-You don't think...?

Japanese: 綾里　真宵が‥‥こ、コロさ
English: Maya's been murd--

Japanese: おねがいします、イトノコ刑事！ ‥‥修験洞が開くまで‥‥
English: Please, Detective! Let's just wait until we can get inside the Sacred Cavern.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタ‥‥ カオが、さらにビリジアンッス。
English: You're... looking kinda viridian there...

Japanese: ‥‥わかったッス。 そんなカオをしないでほしいッス。
English: OK, OK. Just stop it with that face, pal.
One thing, though...

Japanese: このツエのシカケのことは、 だれにもナイショッスよ。
English: This staff's secret trick... Let's keep it between you and me, alright?

Japanese: 知っていてダマっていたとなれば、 アンタも自分もオシマイッス。
English: If it gets out that we knew about it and didn't say anything, we're finished.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。
English: ...I understand.

Japanese: このツエは、あくまで‥‥ “ただのツエ”ということで。
English: This is just a regular, run- of-the-mill staff, you got it?

Japanese: ‥‥じゃ。このツエ、 アンタにあずけておくッス。
English: ...OK, now you take good care of it, pal.

Japanese: す、すみません‥‥
English: Thank you, Detective.

Japanese: きっと‥‥ブジッスよ。
English: Don't worry... She'll be fine.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事‥‥
English: Gumshoe...

Japanese: 山をおりたところに、 おいしいうどん屋があるッス。
English: You know, there's a place at the base of this mountain that has some really good pasta.

Japanese: 綾里　真宵が保護されたら、 連れていってあげるッス。
English: How about I take you there when you find Maya, huh, pal?

Japanese: ありがとうございます‥‥
English: Sure... Thanks...

Japanese: 証拠品《被害者のツエ》を イトノコ刑事から受け取った。
English: Victim's Staff received from Detective Gumshoe for safe- keeping.

Japanese: （それにしても‥‥）
English: (I can't help but wonder, though...)

Japanese: （エリス先生はなぜ、 こんなものを‥‥？）
English: (What was Elise Deauxnim doing with a staff like this in the first place...?)

Japanese: 綾里　供子像が持っている、 あの七支刀‥‥
English: The Shichishito. It's there in Mystic Ami's right hand.

Japanese: 今日の法廷で、あれが凶器では ないと立証されたらしい。
English: But it was proven in court today that it wasn't the murder weapon.

Japanese: とりあえず、罪作りなカタナで あることは、まちがいないな。
English: Given the design, it must've been used for some pretty gruesome things in the past...

Japanese: 境内には、いくつか 灯ろうが並んでいる。
English: There are a few of these stone lanterns scattered around in this courtyard.

Japanese: 街灯がない山の中では、小さい 灯りでも、とても明るく感じる。
English: Without the city glare to compete with, I imagine they would seem a lot brighter.

Japanese: あやめさんがいなくなって‥‥ ビキニさんが灯を入れるのかな。
English: I guess Sister Bikini must light them now, since Iris can't be here.

Japanese: ぼくたちがお世話になっている 葉桜院の本堂だ。
English: That building up there is the Main Hall. It was where we were staying.

Japanese: 急な斜面に建てられているため、 こんなことになってるけど‥‥
English: It sits kind of funny because it was built on a very steep slope.

Japanese: 目の上に見ている部屋は、 山門側から地つづきの、１階だ。
English: If you enter from the Main Gate side, these rooms above me are on the ground floor.

Japanese: カドの部屋は、亡くなった エリス先生が泊まっていたらしい。
English: Apparently, Ms. Deauxnim was staying in the corner room.

Japanese: あそこから、突き落とされた ‥‥のだろうか？
English: I guess that's where she was pushed from that night...

Japanese: 吾童山中は、かなり 雪がつもるから、
English: They get quite a bit of snow up here on Eagle Mountain.

Japanese: スキーは遊び道具ではなく、 移動手段なのかもしれない。
English: Around here, skis aren't just for fun. They're tools for getting around.

Japanese: ‥‥ソリは、ビキニさんが 遊ぶのだろう。
English: But the sled... Now that's gotta be for Bikini's own personal entertainment!

Japanese: 石垣の上は、山門に通じている。
English: The Main Gate's on the other side of that stone wall.

Japanese: 段差は３メートル近くある。 山の斜面が、それだけ急なのだ。
English: The difference in elevation is almost 10 feet. That's how steep this hillside is!

Japanese: エリス先生の身体は、３メートル ぐらいの高さから落ちたらしい。
English: And that's also the height the body fell from, according to the autopsy.

Japanese: もしかしたら、あそこかも しれない‥‥かな？
English: Is it possible that Ms. Deauxnim was pushed from the top of the wall...?

Japanese: エリス先生が持っていた、ツエ。
English: Ms. Deauxnim's staff...

Japanese: たしかに、２日前はアタマに 水晶がついていた。
English: I definitely remember there being a crystal sphere attached to the staff.

Japanese: その水晶は、矢張が橋のたもとで 拾ったらしいけど‥‥
English: I guess Larry eventually found the sphere near the foot of Dusky Bridge, but...

Japanese: どうして、そんなところに 落ちていたんだろう‥‥？
English: ...what was it doing all the way out there...?

Japanese: それで‥‥どうなんですか？ 捜査の状況は。
English: So how's the investigation going, Detective Gumshoe?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタには言いにくいッス。 でも、あえて言っちまうッス。
English: I don't know if I should be telling you, pal. But I guess it can't hurt.

Japanese: やはり‥‥殺人現場は、 奥の院の中庭らしいッス。
English: Looks like the murder took place in the Inner Temple garden.

Japanese: 捜査本部は、あの灯ろうの 血文字を重要と考えているッス。
English: And they're taking that bloody writing on the lantern pretty seriously back at HQ.

Japanese: ！　‥‥それ、どういう イミですか‥‥？
English: What!? You mean...?

Japanese: ‥‥詳しいことは わからないッス。ただ‥‥
English: I don't have the details, pal.

Japanese: もし、修験洞から綾里　真宵が 発見されなかった場合‥‥
English: All I know is, if you don't find Maya down in that Sacred Cavern...

Japanese: サイアクの事態も考えなければ ならないッス。
