English: ...we're gonna be faced with one ugly situation.

Japanese: さ、“サイアクの事態”‥‥ なな、なんですか、それはッ！
English: What kind of ugly situation are we looking at here...?

Japanese: 何も思い浮かばないッス。
English: You got me! I don't know.

Japanese: 自分が言ったワケじゃ ないッスからねェ‥‥。
English: They're not my words, pal.

Japanese: いったい、ダレが！
English: Then who said it?

Japanese: ゴドー検事ッス。
English: Mr. Godot.

Japanese: ご、ゴドー検事‥‥ （どういうつもりだ‥‥？）
English: Godot!? (What did he mean by that?)

Japanese: “本物の”凶器、ですか‥‥
English: So what do you know about the real murder weapon so far?

Japanese: 被害者に刺さっていた七支刀は、 凶器じゃなかった‥‥
English: Well it wasn't the Shichishito that was found impaled in the victim's body.

Japanese: それは、今日の法廷で 御剣検事が立証したッス。
English: Mr. Edgeworth proved that in court today.

Japanese: ‥‥そうらしいですね。
English: ...Yes, that's true.

Japanese: そうなると‥‥もう１本、 別のカタナがあるはずッス。
English: In which case, it must've been another blade.

Japanese: それを、探してるわけですか。 ‥‥あてもなく。
English: And that's what you're running around like a headless chicken looking for now, huh...

Japanese: さすがにつかれたッス。 ‥‥でもッ！
English: Yeah. And man, is it tiring work! But let me tell you something, pal...

Japanese: あてもなく探してた ワケじゃないッス！
English: I'm no chicken.

Japanese: ‥‥これからは、科学捜査の 時代ッスからねぇ‥‥
English: We've got the feather of forensics in our cap these days.

Japanese: 警視庁のほこる、最新鋭の 秘密兵器を使っているッス！
English: We're using the department's secret weapon on this!

Japanese: ひ、ひみつへいき‥‥？
English: Secret weapon...?

Japanese: ‥‥被害者のツエの中に、 カタナが‥‥
English: I'd never have guessed there'd be a sword concealed in the victim's staff.

Japanese: 《仕込みヅエ》というヤツッスね。
English: They call this kind of thing a sword-cane, pal.

Japanese: なんとまあ、美しい細工‥‥‥ これぞまさに“ビサイク”ッス！
English: This one's a real gem... The workmanship is really something else!

Japanese: ビサイクとブサイク‥‥‥ これぞまさに、紙一重ッス！
English: Thank goodness it wasn't a cane-sword, or else the victim would've stabbed her own foot!
(...I officially give up on trying to figure out how Gumshoe's mind works.)

Japanese: （本当の殺人現場が 奥の院の中庭なら‥‥）
English: (If the real scene of the crime was the Inner Temple Garden...)

Japanese: （なぜ、凶器のカタナが 葉桜院の境内にあるんだ‥‥？）
English: (Then why was the sword used to kill the victim found in the Main Hall Courtyard?)

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事。 このツエですけど‥‥
English: Hey, Gumshoe...

Japanese: 中に仕込まれたカタナのことを 知っている人は‥‥？
English: Who knows about the hidden sword?

Japanese: それは、ダレもいないッス！
English: No one.

Japanese: たった今、自分が発見するまで、 警察の人間すら知らなかったッス。
English: Even the police didn't know about this until I discovered it just now.

Japanese: （‥‥発見したの、 ぼくなんだけどな）
English: (Well, as they say, there's no "Team" in "Gumshoe".)

Japanese: 見たところ‥‥血痕は 残っていないようですね。
English: It doesn't look like there are any traces of blood on it.

Japanese: じゃあ、凶器じゃないッスね。 コイツは。
English: Then I guess this isn't the murder weapon, huh?

Japanese: いやいやいや！　犯行後に ふき取ったかもしれないでしょう！
English: No, no, no! I'm sure someone just wiped it off after the murder!

Japanese: そりゃ、そッス！ キョーキにキマリッス！
English: Yeah, of course! This thing's definitely the murder weapon! Great job, pal!

Japanese: （長さも、七支刀と同じぐらい。 ‥‥まちがいない！）
English: (It's about the same length as the Shichishito, too. This must be the murder weapon!)

Japanese: あの‥‥怪文書、というのは？
English: About that letter you found...

Japanese: まったく！　自分が探してるのは 凶器のカタナなのに‥‥
English: Don't remind me, pal! I'm busting a gut here trying to find the murder weapon...

Japanese: 見つかったのは、 こんな燃えカスだけッス。
English: ...and all I find is some burnt up old letter.

Japanese: 燃えカス‥‥
English: Burnt up old letter?

Japanese: 奥の院の前にある、 焼却炉の中にあったッス。
English: Yeah. It was in that incinerator right outside the Inner Temple.

Japanese: ‥‥焼却炉！
English: The incinerator!?

Japanese: （や、やっぱり‥‥ 気のせいじゃなかった！）
English: (I knew it! I knew I wasn't imagining it!)

Japanese: （初めて見たとき、あの焼却炉には 雪がつもっていた）
English: (There was snow on the incinerator when I first saw it.)

Japanese: （それが‥‥事件のあと、 雪はキレイにとけていた‥‥）
English: (But after the incident, the snow had melted away.)

Japanese: （事件当夜‥‥あの焼却炉で、 何かが焼かれたんだ！）
English: (Which means someone used the incinerator on the night of the murder.)

Japanese: ‥‥どうしたッス？ コブシをにぎりしめて‥‥
English: What's up with you, pal? I've got it right here if you wanna take a look.

Japanese: ちょ‥‥ちょっと それ、見せてもらえますか？
English: C-Can I...? Do you mind?

Japanese: いやいや、あげるッスよ、 こんな紙クズ。
English: Sure, go nuts! I don't want it, pal. You can have it.

Japanese: 霊媒だの、家元だの‥‥ 事件にはカンケイないッスからね。
English: It's all spirit mediums and Masters and stuff. I bet it's got no relation to the case.

Japanese: （れ‥‥れいばい‥‥！）
English: (Spirit mediums!?)

Japanese: 証拠品《燃えカス》を 法廷記録にそっと挟んだ。
English: Burnt Letter added to the Court Record.

Japanese: （“霊媒”‥‥“家元”‥‥ キーワードがタダゴトじゃない）
English: (It's got to be important if it mentions spirit mediums and Masters.)

Japanese: （よく目を通して‥‥ そして、考えないと！）
English: (I'd better give it a good looking over and some serious thought...)

Japanese: ちゃんと、ゴミ箱に 捨てておくッスよ！
English: Make sure you chuck it in the trash when you're done. Littering's a crime, pal!

Japanese: なんですか？　秘密兵器って。
English: What's this secret weapon of yours?

Japanese: いッスか？　アンタもひとつ、 科学的に考えてみるッス。
English: You wanna know? You've gotta think scientifically, OK?

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Alright.

Japanese: 凶器は“カタナ”ッス。
English: The murder weapon was a sword.

Japanese: カタナは、科学的に言って いわゆる、金属ッス。
English: Swords are, scientifically speaking, made of metal, right?

Japanese: ここまでで、質問は？
English: Any questions so far?

Japanese: ‥‥ありません。 （もう、コタエもわかったような）
English: No. (I know what he's gonna say, but I'll let him look smart.)

Japanese: そこで、これッス！ ‥‥金属探知機。
English: So what's the perfect tool for the job!? Ta-da! A metal detector!

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥）
English: (Raise your hand if you didn't see this coming from a mile away...)

Japanese: どッスか？　アンタもひとつ、 科学的にやってみるッス。
English: Well? You wanna give scientific investigation a go?

Japanese: はあ。‥‥え！ ぼ、ぼくがやるんですか？
English: Huh? M-Me?

Japanese: いやー、さっきからずうっと やってるッスから‥‥
English: I've been using this thing for hours now.

Japanese: さすがに、あきたッス。 これも、いい経験だと思うッスよ。
English: It gets pretty boring after a while. Why don't you give it a try?

Japanese: （どうする‥‥？）
English: (I don't know...)

Japanese: （イトノコ刑事の お手伝い‥‥やってみるか？）
English: (Should I help Detective Gumshoe out, or not?)

Japanese: さささ、やってみるッス。 楽しいッスよ、捜査は。
English: Come on, pal. It's good fun, I'm telling you.

Japanese: いや、あの。ちょっとぼく、 ヤクソクがありますから‥‥
English: No. I, um... I've actually gotta be someplace...

Japanese: アレ。そッスか。 ‥‥ザンネンッス。
English: Oh, really? That's too bad.

Japanese: じゃあ、自分はここで ちょっと休んでいるッスから。
English: I'll just take a break by myself for a while, then.

Japanese: イジでも、アンタに やらせてみせるッス！
English: I'm gonna get you to do this somehow, pal! You'll see!

Japanese: （捜査が大好きなんじゃ なかったのかよ！）
English: (I thought you liked doing field work! Isn't that what you were just singing about!?)

Japanese: さささ、やってみるッス。 楽しいッスよ、捜査は。
English: Come on, pal. It's good fun, I'm telling you.

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ‥‥ おコトバにあまえて。
English: Alright, then... I guess I'll give it a go...

Japanese: なにぶん、警察の最新鋭の 科学の先っぽの装置ッスから。
English: Like I said, this is the department's most advanced gadget. The absolute best!

Japanese: イヤになるほど高性能ッス！ 気にさわるほどビンカンッス！
English: It's so sensitive, you could make it cry! It's so hi-tech, you could sky dive off it!

Japanese: ‥‥はあ。
English: Oh, bother.

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、説明するッス。
English: So, now I'm gonna tell you how to use it.

Japanese: ここには、おそらく 本当の凶器が眠っているッス。
English: It's possible the real murder weapon is around here somewhere, right?

Japanese: それを探し出すわけですね。
English: Sure. That's what we're trying to find out.

Japanese: ‥‥まず、探知機の スイッチを入れるッス。
English: Right. So first, let's turn the detector on.

Japanese: ‥‥この音は、探知機が金属に 反応して鳴っているッス。
English: That's the sound of the metal detector's signal bouncing off of something metallic.

Japanese: 探知機にタッチして、イロイロな 場所を、よーくさがしてみるッス。
English: Next, touch the detector and take a real good look around this courtyard with it, pal.

Japanese: 金属があると、《ＣＨＥＣＫ》 パネルの表示が変わるッス。
English: Once you've hit something metallic, the CHECK gauge will flash.

Japanese: そこでパネルをタッチすれば、 それを調べることができるッス。
English: And when that happens, touch the gauge to really give the area a good, hard stare.

Japanese: 目に見えないところにも 反応するッスから‥‥
English: This thing picks up metallic objects that are hidden from sight, too...

Japanese: アヤシイところは、 ゼンブ調べてみるッス！
English: Take a good look at anything and everything that seems suspicious, OK pal!?

Japanese: ‥‥ベツに、 変わったものはないな。
English: Hmm... It doesn't look like there's anything here.

Japanese: アンタ！　《ＣＨＥＣＫ》パネルを よく見て探すッスよ！
English: Hey, pal! You've gotta watch the CHECK gauge and wait for it to flash, OK!?

Japanese: （ちょっとヨワいんだよな ‥‥キカイは）
English: (Alright, already... I'm not exactly a wiz at machinery, you know...)

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、マルいものが 埋まってるぞ‥‥）
English: (Looks like there's something round buried in the ground here...)

Japanese: そ、ソイツにさわっちゃ ダメッスッ！
English: Don't touch that, pal!

Japanese: なんですか？　これ。
English: Huh? What is it?

Japanese: ワレワレ刑事を退治するための、 お、おそろしいワナッス。
English: It's a trap! People set them out to catch us detectives. They're real nasty.

Japanese: け、刑事さんを‥‥？
English: A trap... for detectives?

Japanese: もしかして‥‥エモノの足を 鉄のツメで挟む、あのワナですか？
English: You mean like those fierce traps with sharp teeth that are supposed to snag your leg?

Japanese: それッス！　ワナッス！ 自分も、人差し指を挟んだッス！
English: Yeah! Those! I got my finger caught in one of those once.

Japanese: ひとさしゆび‥‥？
English: Y-Your finger?

Japanese: さりげなく置かれたチーズに 手を伸ばした瞬間だったッス。
English: There was this bit of cheese, and it looked so tasty. I reached out, and then... bam!

Japanese: （‥‥なんと言っていいのか わからないな、もう‥‥）
English: (...I'm not sure I know how to respond to a story as stupidly pitiful as that.)

Japanese: これは‥‥ボールペン、かな。
English: What's this...? A pen?

Japanese: あッ！　それ！ 探してたッス！
English: Hey! I've been looking all over for that, pal!

Japanese: い‥‥イトノコ刑事のですか？
English: Huh? It's yours?

Japanese: 刑事になったとき、オヤジが プレゼントしてくれたッス。
English: Yeah. My old man gave it to me when I qualified as a detective.

Japanese: 容疑者を取り調べるとき、これを 鼻先につきつけてやれ、って。
English: He told me it'd be good for sticking in suspects' faces.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、イヤな刑事像ですね。
English: That makes you detectives sound really nasty.

Japanese: いつか、アンタの鼻先に つきつけるのが、自分の夢ッス。
English: It's my dream to stick it in your face one day, Wright!

Japanese: （じゃあ、落とすなよ‥‥）
English: (Then don't go losing it!)

Japanese: ‥‥５００円玉、見つけた。
English: Hey, I found a dollar coin.

Japanese: お、お金を拾ったら 交番に‥‥刑事に届けるッス！
English: Huh? M-Money!? If you find money, you have to turn it in to the police. Or a detective.

Japanese: 半年以内に落とし主が現れなかった ら、１割はアンタのものッス！
English: If the owner doesn't come forward within six months, you get to keep 10 percent!

Japanese: そして、残りの９割は自分の‥‥ ケーサツのものッス！
English: The other ninety percent goes to m... I mean, to the Police Department.

Japanese: そ、そんなにカオを 近づけないでくださいよ。
English: D-Don't get so close to me, Detective. Give me some space, will you!?
Here's the coin. Happy?

Japanese: ‥‥刑事になってよかったッス。
English: I definitely made the right decision becoming a detective.

Japanese: （‥‥なんか、ユガんでないか？ イトノコ刑事の知識‥‥）
English: (Yikes... Talk about a serious case of denial...)

Japanese: ‥‥これは‥‥サイフ、だな。
English: This looks like... a wallet?

Japanese: あああああッ！ そんなところにあったッス！
English: Ah-haaaa! That's where it's been!

Japanese: イトノコ刑事のですか、これ。
English: Is it yours, Detective?

Japanese: よくサイフを落とすから、 スズをつけてあるッス。
English: I'm always dropping it, so I put a bell on it.

Japanese: でも、こうして 落としてるじゃないですか。
English: But you still dropped it anyway...

Japanese: でも、こうして 見つかったッス！
English: Yeah, but now I found it again, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥このサイフ、 カラッポですよ。
English: ...Your wallet is completely empty.

Japanese: よく落とすから、 お金は入れてないッス。
English: Well, I drop it all the time, so I don't put money in it anymore.

Japanese: じゃあ、なんで金属探知機に 反応したんでしょうね。
English: Then how come the metal detector picked it up...?

Japanese: このスズッスね、きっと。
English: Must be because of the bell, I guess.

Japanese: これは‥‥ものほしザオ、か。 金属製だからなあ。
English: This must be an old pan handle or something.

Japanese: な‥‥なんで知ってるッスゥッ！
English: H-How do you know that!?

Japanese: は、はい‥‥？
English: Huh, what?

Japanese: それは‥‥中学校のころの、 自分のニックネームッス！
English: That was my nickname in junior high.

Japanese: ものほしザオ、ですか‥‥？
English: What, "pan handle"?

Japanese: 自分は、おこづかいが 少なくて‥‥
English: I didn't have much money back then, pal.

Japanese: いつも、ものほしそうな カオをしていたッスからねー。
English: I used to bum stuff off the other kids sometimes, so they called me "Panhandler".

Japanese: 《ものほしガオ》ですか。 ‥‥もしかして。
English: "Panhandler", huh? I can see that.

Japanese: 今となっては、ある種 なつかしい思い出ッス。
English: Thinking back, it's actually kind of a nice memory now.

Japanese: ‥‥このソリ、プラスチックで できているのに‥‥
English: That's odd. This sled's made of plastic...

Japanese: 《びきに号》って 書いてあるッスね‥‥
English: It says, "Bikini #1" on the side...

Japanese: （ソリのウラに、 何か落ちているのか‥‥？）
English: (Maybe there's something under it...)

Japanese: こ‥‥これは‥‥ 手帳、かな‥‥。
English: Hey, what's this? A-A badge...?

Japanese: ああああああああッ！ ずーっと探してたッス！
English: Ah-haaaaaa! I've been hunting high and low for that!

Japanese: い、イトノコ刑事のですか？
English: I-It's yours, Detective?

Japanese: イノチより大事な ケーサツ手帳ッス！
English: That thing's priceless to a guy like me, pal. A cop can't be a cop without his badge!

Japanese: 念のために１０円玉を挟んで おいて、よかったッス。
English: I'm sure glad I put that dime in there now.

Japanese: なんで、こんなところに 警察手帳を落とせるんですか！
English: How did you manage to drop your badge here, of all places!?

Japanese: あ！　きっと、あのときッス！
English: I know! I bet it happened this morning.

Japanese: 今朝、このソリで 遊んでいたとき‥‥
English: I was playing around with the sled and...

Japanese: ‥‥もう、いいです。
English: Stop right there. I don't need to hear any more.

Japanese: ‥‥スキー、か。 手がかりにはなりませんね。
English: It's just a pair of skis. Nothing useful for our case here.

Japanese: こう見えても自分、スキーは なかなかのウデ前ッスよ。
English: I'm actually pretty good on skis you know, pal.

Japanese: ゲレンデを、コートをハタめかせて すべり落ちて行くッス！
English: I just love to fly down the slopes with my coat fluttering in the wind!

Japanese: へえ‥‥。どれぐらい すべれるんですか？
English: Really? I had no idea. How good are you, then?

Japanese: そッスねー‥‥
English: Well...

Japanese: スケートより、やや得意ッスが、 スノーボードほどではないッス。
English: I'm better on skis than I am on skates. But not as good as when I'm on a snowboard.

Japanese: （‥‥細かいな）
English: (That's a pretty detailed answer.)

Japanese: あ。でも、イチバン得意なのは カレーッス。
English: Oh! But you know what I'm best at? Making mashed potatoes and gravy.

Japanese: （コンランしてきた。 捜査に戻ろう‥‥）
English: (The guy's losing it. Must be the altitude. We'd better get back to the investigation...)

Japanese: お。ハシゴッス。
English: Look, a ladder!

Japanese: キャタツですよ、それ。
English: That's a "step"-ladder.

Japanese: どうちがうッス？ 似たようなものッスよ。
English: What's the difference? Looks like a normal ladder to me, pal.

Japanese: （‥‥意外と知られてないのか？ このシロモノの名前‥‥）
English: (Surely everyone knows the difference... I mean, they're pretty ordinary objects...)

Japanese: よくいるッス。そういう、 細かいコトにこだわる連中。
English: I've met plenty of guys like you, always picking on the smallest details.

Japanese: ウチの近所の八百屋の オヤジさんも、そッス。
English: The vegetable store guy near my place does it all the time.

Japanese: 『このレタスください』と言ったら 『それはキャベツだよ』なんて。
English: He even corrects me when I ask for a head of lettuce. "That's a cabbage," he says.

Japanese: 自分に言わせりゃ、 どっちも同じッス！
English: I'm telling you, they're the exact same thing!

Japanese: ゼンゼンちがいますよ！
English: No they're not! They're completely different!

Japanese: キャタツとキャベツだって、 ケッキョク、１字のちがいッス！
English: You have to plant both of them firmly in the ground before they can grow, don't you?

Japanese: もっと、ホンシツを見なきゃ、 でっかい手がかりを見逃すッスよ。
English: Listen. You gotta take a step back and look at the bigger picture sometimes.

Japanese: これは、センパイとしての アドバイスッス！
English: Otherwise you could miss a really important clue. That's advice from a pro, pal!

Japanese: （‥‥このヒトに言われたくない）
English: (...The last person I need advice from is this guy in front of me.)

Japanese: 黄金像が持っている七支刀は、 ホンモノの凶器じゃないッスよ。
English: That Shichishito next to the golden statue wasn't the real murder weapon, pal.

Japanese: え、ええ。知ってますよ。 念のため、調べてみたんです。
English: Yeah, I know. I just thought I'd check it again anyway.

Japanese: あ、そうだ！ アンタ、知ってるッスか？
English: Oh, yeah! That reminds me! Do you know about that sword?

Japanese: このカタナは、エダわかれ した、人生をあらわしているッス。
English: It's supposed to represent the multiple branches that life can take, all ending as one.

Japanese: 聞いたことはあります。
English: Yes. I heard something about that.

Japanese: ‥‥ときどき、自分は思うッス。
English: You know what I think about sometimes?

Japanese: 殺人課の刑事にならなかったら、 どんな人生があっただろう、と‥‥
English: What kind of life I'd be leading had I not joined the Homicide Division.

Japanese: ‥‥イトノコ刑事でも、 そんなコトを思うんですか。
English: You think about that kind of stuff, Detective?

Japanese: モチロンッス。 いろいろ、想像するッス。
English: Of course I do. I think about it a lot.

Japanese: 交通課の自分。警視総監の自分。 窃盗課の自分。タイホくんの自分。
English: Me as a traffic cop. Me as a detention officer. Me as the Blue Badger...

Japanese: （‥‥タイホくんのイトノコ刑事は 一見の価値があるな）
English: (...There's something to be said about the humor value of Gumshoe as the Blue Badger.)

Japanese: （‥‥ひさしの上、か。 まさか、あそこに‥‥？）
English: (Is there something up on the eaves?)

Japanese: ここからじゃ、見えないッス。 ‥‥ま。あきらめるッスね。
English: I can't tell from here, pal. ...Meh, just forget about it.

Japanese: でも、エリス先生の部屋のマドの すぐ下ですよ！
English: But it's right under Elise Deauxnim's window!

Japanese: 凶器のカタナをかくすのに、 ピッタリじゃないですか！
English: And it's the perfect spot for hiding something long and thin like a sword!

Japanese: ちょっと、行ってくるッス！
English: I'll go take a look.

Japanese: いやー、よかったッス。 スプーンが落ちてただけッス。
English: Ah, it was nothing, thank goodness. Someone just dropped a spoon.

Japanese: ‥‥なんか、ホッとしてませんか？
English: Why are you so happy about that...?

Japanese: そりゃそッス。もし、いきなり あそこに凶器があったら‥‥
English: Well, imagine if the murder weapon had turned up there...

Japanese: 自分が長ながと説明したのが バカみたいッスからねー。
English: It'd make me look pretty dumb, huh?

Japanese: （‥‥まあ、そうだな）
English: (...At least he recognizes that much.)

Japanese: これは‥‥エリスさんの ツエ、ですよね。
English: This is Ms. Deauxnim's staff, isn't it?

Japanese: 科学的に考えると‥‥ ツエは、木でできてるッス。
English: Scientifically speaking, they're usually made of wood.

Japanese: でも、ジッサイに 反応してるじゃないですか。
English: But the detector is reacting to it.

Japanese: 手ざわりから何から、 どう見ても木ッスがねえ‥‥
English: Yeah, but however you look at it, the thing's made of wood, alright?

Japanese: 自分はハナっから、金属に見える ものしか、調べないッス。
English: I don't bother investigating anything unless it looks like it might be metallic.

Japanese: （‥‥探知機を使うイミが ないじゃないか、それ）
English: (Isn't the whole point of a metal detector to find metal where you can't see it?)

Japanese: まさか、こんなものに 反応するとは‥‥
English: It's weird that this thing is causing a racket...

Japanese: ちょっと、貸してくださいよ。
English: Here, let me take a look at it.

Japanese: な、ナニするッス！　ここは おまわりさんにまかせて‥‥
English: Hey! Hands off! Examining evidence is a job for the...
*crack*

Japanese: ‥‥あ。アタマの部分が‥‥ 取れちゃいましたよ！
English: Ack! The top is coming off!

Japanese: あ、アンタ！　ダイジな証拠品を へし折るなんて、なんたる‥‥
English: Look what you've done! You've damaged a really important piece of evidence.

Japanese: ‥‥うわああああああッ！
English: Aaaaaaaaah!

Japanese: ツエの中から‥‥
English: Th-That's a...

Japanese: か‥‥カタナ‥‥
English: A... A sword...

Japanese: ま‥‥まさか‥‥
English: Inside the staff...

Japanese: こ‥‥これが‥‥
English: Is this... Could it be...

Japanese: きょ‥‥‥‥‥‥キョーキ‥‥ッ！
English: ...The murder weapon!?

Japanese: ナゾのミステリーに 包まれているッス。
English: That guy's got more mysteries than a Choose Your Own Escapade book, pal.

Japanese: それはもう聞きましたよ。
English: Yeah, I know. Trust me.

Japanese: まったく‥‥今日も 法廷を無断ですっぽかして。
English: I can't believe he skipped the trial like that, without even getting permission.

Japanese: これがフツウの会社なら、 クビッスよ！
English: He'd have been fired for sure if he worked for a normal company.

Japanese: それで、ゴドー検事は今、 どこにいるんですか？
English: Do you know where he is now?

Japanese: 知らねッス。コーヒー片手に、 その辺をほっつき歩いているッス。
English: Nah. But I bet he's walking around somewhere with a mug of coffee in one hand.

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥イトノコ刑事は 知らないみたいだな）
English: (Hmm, I should've figured...)

Japanese: （ゴドー検事の身体のこと‥‥）
English: (It looks like Gumshoe doesn't know about Godot's physical condition...)

Japanese: 死人が地獄からよみがえるには‥‥ くだらねえ手つづきが必要なのさ。
English: Of course, being extradited from Hell is a tedious affair.

Japanese: 定期的な身体機能の精密検査、 体内物質の補給・調整‥‥
English: The meticulous regeneration and adjustment of all your internal organs is...

Japanese: めんどくせえ現代医学にすがって、 みっともなく生きながらえる‥‥
English: Well... Let's just say modern medicine allows us all to live to a ripe old age.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、今の、このオレだ。
English: Even someone like me.

Japanese: （法廷を休んだ理由は キチンとしているのに‥‥）
English: (He had a good reason for missing the trial.)

Japanese: （‥‥どうして、それを かくしているんだろう）
English: (So why didn't he tell anyone about it?)

Japanese: ムカシ‥‥一度だけ、 尋問を受けたことがあるッス。
English: She cross-examined me once, you know.

Japanese: あれは‥‥名勝負だったッス。
English: Yeah. That was us. The big guns locking horns.

Japanese: 証人対弁護士！　アツいアツい カケヒキが展開されたッス。
English: Witness versus lawyer! It was a battle of cunning tactics and tricks.

Japanese: （証人がカケヒキしちゃ マズいだろ‥‥）
English: (But the witness isn't supposed to play any tricks...)

Japanese: おしいヒトを亡くしたッス‥‥。
English: I can't believe she's gone...

Japanese: ‥‥あいかわらず、行方不明ッス。
English: The situation hasn't changed. We still don't know anything about Maya's whereabouts.

Japanese: もう、修験洞の中にいるとしか 考えられないッス。
English: She's gotta be in the Sacred Cavern, though. That's the only place left.

Japanese: かなりサムいらしいッスからね。 早く助けないと、アブナイッス。
English: I heard it's real cold in there. We've gotta get her out fast, or it could be bad, pal.

Japanese: なんとか、できないんですか！
English: Isn't there anything you can do, Detective!?

Japanese: アンタに、そんなコトを 言われるスジアイはないッス！
English: What are you talking about!?
...Huh?

Japanese: ワレワレの対応は、これ以上 考えられないほど、素早いッス！
English: We're already on it! You couldn't get a faster response from a computer.

Japanese: ゴドー検事が、ものすごい速さで 手配してくれたからッス！
English: Mr. Godot put everything in place right away. That guy doesn't mess around.

Japanese: ご‥‥ゴドー検事が‥‥？
English: G-Godot?

Japanese: ナゾのミステリーに包まれた コーヒー好きのチコク魔ッスが‥‥
English: Yeah. The coffee-drinking mystery man who didn't show in court.

Japanese: 今回のことで、自分はちょっぴり あのヒトを見直したッス。
English: I gotta say. He's totally made me rethink my perception of him, pal.

Japanese: おッ！　そうだ。 聞いてくれるッスか！
English: Hey, you wanna hear something, pal? Come on, I know you do!

Japanese: 今度、マコクンが自分に、コートを プレゼントしてくれるッス！
English: Maggey says she's gonna buy me a new coat!

Japanese: へえ！　よかったですね。
English: Really? That's great!

Japanese: こないだの事件の お礼だそうッス！
English: Yeah. I think it's to say thanks for helping her out during that last case!

Japanese: 自分のエキスがしみこんだ、 このカツオ風味のコート‥‥
English: Which means...

Japanese: これでやっと、 ヤッカイ払いできるッス！
English: I can finally chuck out this stinky, sweat-soaked rag!

Japanese: 事件の夜から、奥の院側に 閉じこめられていたらしいッス。
English: The girl was trapped over at the Inner Temple since the night of the murder.

Japanese: 真宵ちゃんのこと‥‥ 何か言ってませんでしたか？
English: Did she say anything to you about Maya?

Japanese: ‥‥ぼくには、 話してくれなかったんです‥‥
English: She wouldn't tell me...

Japanese: アンタに話さないことを、自分に 教えてくれるワケ、ないッス。
English: She's not gonna tell me something if she wouldn't even speak to you, pal.

Japanese: 泣いているばっかりで、 見ていてツラかったッス。
English: She was real upset. She wouldn't stop crying.

Japanese: やさしくて、いい おばちゃんッス。
English: She's a sweet old lady.

Japanese: とても、ウソをつくような ヒトには見えないッス。
English: She doesn't look like the lying type to me.

Japanese: でも‥‥ビキニさんの証言には、 問題があったみたいですね。
English: But there was a problem with her testimony, wasn't there?

Japanese: あれはきっと、おばちゃんの せいじゃないッス。
English: Yeah, but I don't think it was her fault.

Japanese: ‥‥自分は正しいコトを言ってる つもりが、そうでもなかった‥‥
English: She was just trying to say the right thing, and it came out wrong, that's all.

Japanese: 自分には、わかるッス！ よくあるコトッス！
English: I know what it's like. Happens to me all the time!

Japanese: どうにも、ヒミツの多い 被害者ッス。
English: She sure was a mysterious person.

Japanese: 本名はおろか、今までの 経歴も、完全に不明ッス。
English: Her real name, her past... It's all a complete mystery.

Japanese: （エリス先生が、真宵ちゃんの おかあさんであること‥‥）
English: (Not quite. She was Maya's mother.)

Japanese: （まだ、知らないみたいだな）
English: (But Gumshoe doesn't know that yet...)

Japanese: あいかわらず、 ナゾはミステリーッス。
English: Just like every other case, huh? Always a mystery!

Japanese: ‥‥自分には、自信が なくなってきたッス。
English: I dunno. I'm not so sure anymore.

Japanese: 今日、発見された手がかりで 事件はガラッと変わったッス。
English: The clues we found today have turned this case all topsy-turvy.

Japanese: 正直、彼女が犯人だとは、 考えにくい状況ッス。
English: I can't see how Iris could've done it now.

Japanese: その《手がかり》というのは‥‥？
English: What clues are you talking about?

Japanese: ‥‥アンタには言いにくいッス。 でも、あえて言っちまうッス。
English: I dunno if I should be telling you, pal. But I guess I can let you in on it.

Japanese: 現場が、奥の院の中庭だったことと ‥‥あの、血文字ッス。
English: The fact that the Inner Temple Garden was the crime scene. And... that bloody writing.

Japanese: なにしろ、事件当夜‥‥奥の院には 修行中の綾里　真宵しか‥‥
English: However you look at it, Maya Fey's the only person who...

Japanese: その、御剣検事ばりのスルドい目で ニラむの、やめてほしいッス。
English: Hey! Quit giving me that evil stare, would you? You look just like Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: ‥‥真宵ちゃんのハズ、 ないじゃないですか！
English: It wasn't Maya! There is no way it could've been her!

Japanese: やっぱり、言わなきゃ よかったッス。
English: I knew I shouldn't have said anything.

Japanese: 天流斎　エリス先生の お弟子さんだそうッス。
English: He was Ms. Elise Deauxnim's apprentice, or so I heard.

Japanese: ‥‥そうですね。
English: ...Well, I suppose you could call him that.

Japanese: 似顔絵、描いてもらったッス。
English: The guy drew a portrait of me, you know.

Japanese: 最高じゃないですか。
English: Well, lucky you, I suppose.

Japanese: でも。５０円、とられたッス。
English: Yeah, but I had to pay him 50 cents for it.

Japanese: 最低ですね。
English: That's pretty awful.

Japanese: どうも、気のないそぶりッスね、 アンタも。
English: You aren't really enthused about this guy either, huh...?

Japanese: 今日の裁判も、メチャクチャに してくれたそうですから。
English: Well... he made a mockery of the court today.

Japanese: （たまに、ワザとやってるんじゃ ないかと思うときがあるよ）
English: (And I'm almost sure he did it on purpose, too...)

Japanese: ‥‥そういえば、狩魔検事は どうしたッスか？
English: Hey, speaking of Ms. von Karma, what's she up to...?

Japanese: 奥の院のあやめさんに ついていてもらっています。
English: She's keeping an eye on Iris at the Inner Temple.

Japanese: お。ホントッスか！
English: Oh, really!?

Japanese: ふうう、やれやれ。これで やっと、のんびりできるッス。
English: Phew! I can relax at last; now that you've lost her.

Japanese: イトノコ刑事、いつも たたかれてますからね。ムチで。
English: She has a real thing for hitting you with that whip of hers, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥最近、ちょっと慣れてきたのが 自分でもコワいッス。
English: Yeah, but I'm kinda getting used to it... which scares me a lot.

Japanese: そういえば一度だけ、ムチを 忘れてきたことがあったッスね。
English: Oh! So this one time, she forgot her whip at home or something...

Japanese: へえ。そのときは、 たたかれずにすんだワケですね？
English: And...? Tell me you were able to have a violence-free day.

Japanese: かわりに、ビンタを食らったッス。
English: Nah. She slapped me instead.

Japanese: ムチの方がまだ、やられていて カッコがつくッス。
English: I prefer the whip. Getting slapped all day makes you look like such a loser.

Japanese: （たいして変わらないと 思うけどな）
English: (And being whipped doesn't?)

Japanese: もお、ひさしぶりッス。 １年ぶりぐらいッスかねー。
English: I haven't seen Mr. Edgeworth for about a year. Talk about a long time!

Japanese: アイツ、わざわざ外国で 何やってるんですか？
English: Do you know why he moved overseas?

Japanese: さあ。カッコつけてるんじゃ ないッスか。やっぱり。
English: I don't know. Probably to look cool.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: ハクがつくッスからねー。 海外で研究なんて。
English: There's a lot of weight attached to doing research overseas, you know.

Japanese: 自分も、パリで修行してきたら、 ムッシュ・イトノコギリッスよ。
English: Once I've done my stint in Paris, you'll have to call me Monsieur Gumchaussure.

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ、ブカに完全に リカイされていないな‥‥）
English: (People really don't get Edgeworth, do they?)

Japanese: ‥‥どこかで見たことがあるッス。
English: I've seen this lady someplace before.

Japanese: ‥‥大福をゴチソウに‥‥
English: Isn't she that jaw-droppingly large strawberry desert woman?

Japanese: ああ！　春美ちゃんの おかあさんッスね！
English: Oh, yeah! She's Pearl Fey's mother, right?

Japanese: ええ、そうです。
English: That's right.

Japanese: たしか去年、倉院の里の 事件で‥‥
English: She was involved in that incident in Kurain Village last year, wasn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥‥！ ハンニンだったヒト、ッス。
English: Hey! She was the one who did it!

Japanese: （最後まで思い出してから しゃべればいいのに）
English: (Why doesn't he think his whole thought through BEFORE opening his mouth!?)

Japanese: ‥‥あれは、ホントに イヤな事件だったッス。
English: Yeah, that was one ugly case, huh, pal?

Japanese: （“イヤな事件だった”‥‥か。 もしかしたら‥‥）
English: ("Was"? I don't know...)

Japanese: （その事件は、まだ終わっていない のかもしれない‥‥）
English: (The more we look at this case, the more I feel like that one never ended...)

Japanese: ま。今はキチンと 刑務所にいるはずッスから、
English: She's locked up safe in solitary confinement at the detention center, though...

Japanese: 今回の事件とはマッタク、 無関係ッスけどね！
English: At least we can rule her out of this case!

Japanese: ほら。弁護士のバッジです。
English: Hey, look. It's my attorney's badge.

Japanese: はっはっ。あいかわらず サエないバッジッスね。
English: Ha ha. Good one, pal. You're always good for a laugh with your little badge.

Japanese: やっぱり男は、 ダマって警察手帳ッス。
English: But a real man... A real man has a police badge.

Japanese: お。キレイッス！　もしかして、 キャンデーッスか？
English: Hey, that looks good. Is it a cough drop?

Japanese: ちょうどノドが痛かったッス。
English: I tell you, pal. I've had a sore throat all day.

Japanese: ちち、ちがいますよ！ 勾玉です、勾玉！
English: Ack! No! That's my Magatama! You can't eat it!

Japanese: ‥‥なんだ。ちがうッスか。
English: Oh...

Japanese: （いきなり口に放りこむ クセは、なおったらしいな）
English: (At least he doesn't just put stuff in his mouth first and ask questions later anymore.)

Japanese: この掛け軸なんですけど‥‥ 何か知ってますか？
English: Do you know anything about this scroll, Detective?

Japanese: お。うまそうな ニオイがするッス！
English: Well, I know it smells great!

Japanese: いやー、こんな日は やっぱり、カレーッスねえ。
English: Yeah, this is just the kind of weather for some steaming mashed potatoes and gravy.

Japanese: カレーといえば、やっぱり ウインナーカレーッス！
English: Oh! And weenies! Mashed potatoes with gravy and weenies!

Japanese: カレーとウインナーという、 ２大ゴチソウの夢の競演！
English: Potatoes and weenies are the two greatest foods invented by man. A dream combination!

Japanese: ‥‥自分の、見果てぬ夢ッス。
English: Although, I can't afford to try them together yet. It's an unfulfilled dream...

Japanese: あの‥‥カレーはともかく、 この掛け軸ですけど‥‥
English: Um... I was hoping you'd have more to say about the scroll and less about the gravy...

Japanese: そういうのは自分、ちょっと わからねッス。
English: Oh, sorry. Nah, I can't tell you much about that scroll, pal.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (...Why do I bother anymore?)

Japanese: 被害者が身につけていたと 考えて、まちがいないッス！
English: That thing must've been worn by the victim. There's no other way of looking at it.

Japanese: 被害者のクビに、革のヒモが 引っかかっていた、とか。
English: You mean, because of the matching leather cord found around the victim's neck?

Japanese: そッス。きっと、いつも 身につけていたッスね。
English: Yeah. I bet it was one of those things she always wore.

Japanese: 自分も去年から、交通安全の 《お守り》を持ってるッス。
English: I've got this safe driving charm I've been wearing for over a year now.

Japanese: これで自分が事故にあったら、 アイツのせいッス。
English: If I ever get into an accident, it guarantees it'll be the other guy's fault.

Japanese: （そういうものじゃ ないだろう、《お守り》って）
English: (Isn't it supposed to stop you from having an accident in the first place?)

Japanese: 自分には、ムズカシイことは よくわからないッスが‥‥
English: I didn't follow the ins and outs of it, but...

Japanese: ケッキョク、これは キョーキじゃなかったッスね？
English: ...it turned out that this wasn't the murder weapon, right?

Japanese: ビキニさんが目撃したのが 正しければ‥‥ですけど。
English: If what Sister Bikini saw is right, then no, it's not.

Japanese: 凶器が、刃の根本まで身体に 刺さったのなら‥‥
English: She said the murder weapon was thrust into the victim up to the hilt.

Japanese: 血痕の残りかたが 不自然ですからね。このカタナは。
English: But the blood stains on this aren't consistent with her story.

Japanese: 大丈夫ッス！　きっと自分が、 キョーキを見つけてみせるッス！
English: Don't worry, pal! I'll uncover the real murder weapon.

Japanese: 春が来るまでにッ！
English: If I haven't turned it up by Spring, you can take my badge!

Japanese: （待てないよ！）
English: (The case will be long over by then, Gumshoe!)

Japanese: 天流斎　エリスのツエ、ッスね。
English: That staff belonged to the victim.

Japanese: 水晶がなくなっている以外、 アヤシイところ、ありませんね。
English: Yeah, and apart from the crystal missing from the top, there's nothing wrong with it.

Japanese: あ、そうだ！ ここだけのハナシがあるッス。
English: Oh, I just remembered something important! This is just between us, OK?

Japanese: な、なんですか？
English: O-OK... What is it?

Japanese: これを言うのは、アンタが 初めてッス。‥‥ヒミツッスよ。
English: I've never told anyone this before... It's a secret.

Japanese: じつは‥‥自分はムカシ‥‥ 魔法使いになりたかったッス。 I...
English: I actually wanted to be a wizard when I was a kid.

Japanese: もしかして‥‥ それで終わりですか？
English: Is that it?

Japanese: 終わりッス。
English: Yeah, that's it.

Japanese: （ツエからはずれた水晶‥‥か）
English: (Hmm... How did the crystal sphere get separated from the staff?)

Japanese: （なんで、おぼろ橋なんかに 落ちていたんだろう‥‥）
English: (And what was it doing near the foot of Dusky Bridge...?)

Japanese: まさか‥‥このツエの中に カタナが仕込まれていたなんて。
English: I still can't believe it, pal! I'd never have guessed there was a sword in that thing.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、血痕は 残っていないッスけど‥‥
English: Sure, there isn't any blood left on it, but...

Japanese: おそらく、コイツが凶器ッス！ 刑事のカンがドンチャン騒ぎッス！
English: ...there's a good chance this is the murder weapon, pal!

Japanese: でも‥‥これ、エリスさんの ツエですよ？
English: But this is Ms. Deauxnim's staff, right?

Japanese: 自分の武器でやられたコトに なりますけど‥‥
English: That would mean she was killed with her own weapon.

Japanese: ま。そうなるッスね。
English: I guess so.

Japanese: それに‥‥もうひとつ、 気になるコトがあります。
English: And there's something else that's bothering me, too.

Japanese: なんスか？ 聞くだけ、聞いてあげるッス。
English: Oh? What's that, pal?

Japanese: 絵本作家のエリス先生が、 なぜ‥‥
English: Ms. Deauxnim was an author and an illustrator, right?

Japanese: こんなカタナを、かくし持って いたんでしょう？
English: So what was she doing carrying a sword around with her?

Japanese: アンタ、いいところに 気がついたッスね。
English: That's a good question.

Japanese: あとは、いいコタエを 見つけるだけッス。
English: And there's gotta be a good answer to it, I guess.

Japanese: アンタ自身の手でッ！
English: Good luck finding it, pal!

Japanese: （‥‥ホント、役に立たない 刑事さんだな‥‥）
English: (...I'm beginning to see why he receives the salary he does.)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 この、現場写真ですけど‥‥
English: What do you make of this photo of the crime scene...?

Japanese: ホント、ヒドい犯人ッスねー。
English: It's really gruesome... All I gotta say is that the killer is messed up in the head.

Japanese: でも、キョーキがこの 七支刀じゃないなら‥‥
English: But it turns out that Shichishito thing wasn't even the murder weapon.

Japanese: どうして、こんなコトを したッスかねえ。
English: So then, why would someone stab it in the victim like that?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥それはナゾだな）
English: (That's the mystery we're here to solve...)

Japanese: 今日の法廷は、もう お祭り騒ぎだったッス。
English: It was like a circus today in court.

Japanese: ‥‥このラクガキのせいで。
English: All thanks to this little sketch.

Japanese: ムシしちゃったほうが いいッスよ。アンタも。
English: You want my advice? You should ignore it, pal.

Japanese: ‥‥たしかに、矢張は あんなオトコだけど‥‥
English: Look, I know Larry's a bit out there.

Japanese: イミもなく、こんなものを 描くようなヤツじゃないんですよ。
English: But he wouldn't just draw something like this as a joke.

Japanese: じゃあ、明日の裁判を 楽しみにしておくッス。
English: Then I guess I should start getting hyped for tomorrow's trial.

Japanese: え。
English: Huh?

Japanese: この絵に、アンタがどんな セツメイをつけるのか‥‥
English: Seeing you trying to explain that thing...

Japanese: コイツは、見逃せないッス！
English: I wouldn't miss it for the world, pal!

Japanese: （‥‥やっぱり、ムシするか。 こんなラクガキ‥‥）
English: (Maybe he's right. Maybe I should just ignore this useless piece of graffiti.)

Japanese: ‥‥おぼろ橋の近くで 見つかったんですよね？
English: This was found close to Dusky Bridge, wasn't it?

Japanese: あの絵描きは、 そう言っているッス。
English: That's what that artist guy said, yeah.

Japanese: 被告人が、燃えるおぼろ橋を ピョーイと飛びこえたあと、
English: Right after the defendant flew over the burning bridge!

Japanese: 着地したときに落とした、とか。
English: According to his testimony, she dropped it when she landed on the other side.

Japanese: （でも‥‥この水晶は、 あやめさんのものじゃない）
English: (But this crystal didn't belong to Iris.)

Japanese: しかも、水晶には 血痕が付着しているッス。
English: There are traces of blood on it too, you know, pal.

Japanese: （そんなものが‥‥なぜ！ おぼろ橋のたもとに‥‥）
English: (The real question is what was it doing near the foot of Dusky Bridge...?)

Japanese: イトノコ刑事。 ‥‥これなんですけど。
English: What about this, Detective Gumshoe? Any thoughts?

Japanese: うーん‥‥そッスねー‥‥。
English: Yeah... Well...

Japanese: ちょっと今日はサムいッスから、 アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: It's pretty chilly today, huh? My brain doesn't work properly in the cold.

Japanese: （夏まで待て、ということか？）
English: Perhaps we'll just have to wait until Summer when it's warmer?

Japanese: あ。自分、アツくても アタマがはたらかないッス。
English: Oh, no. My brain doesn't work properly in the heat.

Japanese: なにぶん、ナイーブッスから。
English: I'm kinda weak in the head.

Japanese: （自分で言うなよ‥‥）
English: (There are some things you're better off not advertising, Gumshoe...)

Japanese: （今はまだ、証拠が 足りないのかもしれない）
English: (I don't think I have enough evidence yet...)

Japanese: （もう少し調査をして、 材料を集めてみるか‥‥）
English: (I should investigate and gather some more clues before I try again...)

Japanese: ‥なるほどくん‥
English: ...Mr. Nick...

Japanese: ‥これ以上つづけると、 ココロがこわれてしまいます‥
English: ...If you push yourself any more, your soul will shatter...

Japanese: ‥もう一度、冷静になって 考えなおしましょう‥
English: ...Please calm down, collect your thoughts, and try again...

Japanese: （‥‥しまった！ シッパイしすぎたか‥‥）
English: (Nnrgh! I've made too many mistakes!)
